The special preferred stock issued to Thramann will carry significant governance rights, including a liquidation preference of at least $20.5 million, alongside flexible conversion and redemption features, giving Thramann substantial influence over the newly formed entity.Both Auddia and Thramann's boards have unanimously approved the merger, with Auddia's independent special committee affirming the deal's fairness, supported by a fairness opinion from Houlihan Capital. Governance continuity is assured, with Auddia's four directors set to join the McCarthy Finney board, while Jeff Thramann and John Mahoney will continue as CEO and CFO, respectively. Additionally, Auddia's executives and directors have committed to support the deal, and Thramann will adhere to a 180-day lock-up on his shares in McCarthy Finney.In terms of valuation, Auddia estimates a base-case worth of $250 million for McCarthy Finney, based on a management-prepared discounted cash flow analysis of the combined entity's pro forma over the next decade. The merger is anticipated to close in the second quarter of 2026, pending Auddia shareholder approval, SEC effectiveness of a Form S-4 filing, and meeting Nasdaq listing requirements. The formation of McCarthy Finney positions the company as a diversified holding entity focused on artificial intelligence applications across distributed AI data centers, healthtech value-based care, and AI-driven travel services.Auddia, based in Boulder, Colorado, is recognized for its innovative audio technology that leverages a proprietary AI platform to enhance consumer engagement with AM/FM radio, podcasts, and other audio content. Its flagship product, the 'faidr' superapp, aims to modernize music consumption and promotion by offering ad-free listening, content and ad-break skipping, and integrated artist discovery.

+ MCCARTHY HOLDINGS ADVANCES SUSTAINABILITY WITH 2024 GHG EMISSIONS REPORT

McCarthy Holdings, one of the largest employee-owned construction firms in the U.S., has released its updated 2024 Greenhouse Gas (GHG) Emissions Report. The report was published on 16th February 2026, and reflects McCarthy's commitment to strengthening its sustainability efforts across its operations and subsidiaries.Developed in partnership with Gravity, a leading enterprise carbon accounting and energy management platform, the report provides a comprehensive view of McCarthy's carbon footprint. It covers Scope 1, Scope 2, and key categories of Scope 3 emissions, with Scopes 1 and 2 having undergone independent auditing and awarded limited assurance. This commitment to transparency and accountability aligns with McCarthy's mission to be the best builder in America.Ian Pope, Senior Director of Sustainability at McCarthy Holdings, emphasized the company's dedication to community building. 'As an employee-owned company, this important effort is in line with our core values and provides our teams with the practical data necessary to meet regulatory requirements,' he stated. McCarthy's sustainability strategy is anchored in three primary priorities: understanding its environmental impact, acting responsibly for its people and communities, and efficiently managing energy consumption.In addition to emissions reporting, McCarthy Holdings integrates responsible practices into its projects. This includes biodiversity initiatives, responsible fuel management, and the use of digital tools and artificial intelligence for smarter project decisions. The company is also focusing on responsible end-of-life management for solar panels through its advanced PV recycling program, partnering with Solar Energy Industries Association (SEIA)-approved recyclers to divert waste from landfills and support a circular economy.By leveraging emissions data and operational insights, McCarthy Holdings continues to demonstrate that its role as a builder of communities extends to fostering a more sustainable future. With a commitment to environmental stewardship and community well-being, McCarthy is setting benchmarks in the construction industry for responsible practices.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC [3 News That Matters]

+ CARLY REINER CACERES JOINS CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP AS HR DIRECTOR

Carly Reiner Caceres has been appointed as the Director of Human Resources – National Campus Recruiting at Clark Construction Group, bringing a wealth of experience in people operations and talent strategy. On the 29th of April 2026, Caceres officially began her new role, where she will be instrumental in supporting national campus recruiting initiatives aimed at bolstering early-career talent pipelines for one of the largest building and infrastructure companies in the United States.Prior to her appointment at Clark Construction Group, Caceres served as the Vice President of People Operations at Proper, where she was pivotal in shaping the HR strategy and operational support within a hybrid work environment. Her extensive background includes significant roles at MILLER as the Director of Internal Operations and more than nine years at Accenture, where she excelled in various capacities including Talent Strategy Manager and Campus Recruiting Lead.Caceres’ career trajectory showcases her expertise in full-cycle recruiting, stakeholder management, and employer branding, which will be crucial as Clark Construction Group seeks to attract top talent from universities across the nation. Her previous experience managing campus recruiting strategies at Grant Thornton LLP further solidifies her capabilities in building robust internship programs and enhancing student engagement initiatives.Clark Construction Group, founded in 1906, has established a strong reputation for delivering value across major markets, including education and infrastructure. With a commitment to community strengthening and project excellence, the company is poised to benefit from Caceres' strategic insights and leadership in HR, particularly in nurturing the next generation of professionals in the construction industry.Caceres’ early career involved internships and roles with various organizations, including Charleston Magazine and the Smithsonian Institution’s Hirshhorn Museum, which helped her build a diverse skill set in communications and external relations. As she embarks on this new chapter at Clark Construction Group, her multifaceted background is expected to contribute significantly to the company's growth and talent development objectives.

+ CLARK CONTRACTS SECURES MULTI-TRADES FRAMEWORK WITH RENFREWSHIRE COUNCIL

Clark Contracts has been appointed to Renfrewshire Council's multi-trades contractors framework, enhancing the delivery of repair, maintenance, and refurbishment services to domestic properties across the local authority area. This announcement was made on 11th February 2026.The framework, which is set to run until 2027, encompasses a variety of multi-trade works. This will enable Renfrewshire Council to effectively manage both responsive and planned maintenance services across its housing stock, ensuring that local residents receive prompt and reliable service.Clark Contracts is already an established player in the public sector, holding several framework agreements that include Scotland Excel’s New Build Residential Framework and NHS National Services Scotland’s Minor Works Framework. Additionally, the company is involved with the Crown Commercial Service’s Construction Framework and various Scottish Procurement Alliance frameworks focusing on Public Buildings, New Build Housing, and more.Gordon Cunningham, managing director at Clark Contracts, expressed enthusiasm about the new appointment, saying, "Building on our strong track record of delivering more than £200 million in refurbishment projects over the past three years, we are pleased to have been appointed to Renfrewshire Council’s framework. As a Paisley-based business, we are proud to be supporting the council and delivering refurbishment and maintenance services that directly benefit local communities."The addition of this framework is expected to bolster Clark Contracts' already significant presence in the public sector, as the company continues to collaborate with various councils and institutions, including the University of Glasgow and Glasgow City Council, to deliver essential services.

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP ADVANCES ₱4-B MIXED-USE HUB AT THE VILLAGES

CLARK FREEPORT – A ₱4-billion mixed-use residential and commercial development in The Villages, a 67-hectare master-planned sports and lifestyle destination here, has been given the green light with the approval by the Clark Development Corp. of an amendment to PKR Corp.’s lease agreement on 13th December 2025. This ambitious project underscores Clark Construction Group's aggressive strategy to attract significant investments and fortify its reputation as Asia’s Leading Meetings and Conference Destination, a title recently bestowed by the World Travel Awards.The revised lease agreement provides PKR Corp. with a 30-month timeline to complete construction and incorporates stringent performance safeguards to ensure timely project delivery. Atty. Agnes VST Devanadera, president and CEO of Clark Development Corp. (CDC), emphasized that the amendment reflects the organization's commitment to fostering investor confidence and efficient execution of projects. "With this agreement, we ensure that it is a good place for everyone to go and with our coordination, we want to be able to maintain that area [The Villages], an area of preference, where people can reside and really be part of the community," Devanadera stated.PKR Corp. president Daekwon Jung expressed gratitude for the partnership with CDC, stating, "We are honored to take part in Clark’s growth and with CDC as our partner, we will deliver this development in Clark." PKR Corp. operates under the umbrella of Kyeryong Construction Industrial Co., Ltd., a South Korean firm known for its expertise in building, civil, residential, and infrastructure projects. The company has received accolades for safety, technology, and workplace practices from various esteemed organizations including the Korea Expressway Corp. and the Ministry of Trade, Industry and Energy.This mixed-use development is part of CDC’s broader strategy to expedite investments, promote well-planned communities, and establish a business-friendly environment that appeals to both global and local investors. The focus on creating integrated lifestyle hubs aligns with the increasing demand for residential options that complement commercial and recreational spaces, positioning Clark as an attractive destination for future developments.Clark Construction Group's proactive approach in securing this project represents a significant step forward in the region's economic landscape, potentially catalyzing further investments and developments. As the construction timeline unfolds, stakeholders will be keenly observing how this initiative contributes to the overall growth and vibrancy of The Villages and the Clark Freeport Zone.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

value.Title: Land Commissioner: Matthew McQueen (D)

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JGH-85N3-S415-P1W3-00000-00Land Commissioner: Matthew McQueen (D)2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:54:50ZLexisNexisELECTIONLand Commissioner: Matthew McQueen (D)

Name: Matthew McQueen

Political Party: Democratic

Age: 58

Education: BA, Williams College; JD, University of Michigan Law School; MS, University of Michigan School of Natural Resources; MBA, University of New Mexico

Occupation: Attorney

City of Residence: Galisteo

Campaign Website: www.mcqueenfornm.com

How has your background prepared you to serve as New Mexico's land commissioner?

With over 30 years' experience practicing real property law (specializing in land conservation), 12 years in the Legislature, and 10 years as Chair of the House Energy, Environment & Natural Resources Committee, everything in my personal, professional, and political background has prepared me to serve as New Mexico's Land Commissioner.

If elected, how would you seek to maximize revenue for schools, universities and other state trust land beneficiaries?

The State Land Office earns over $2 Billion a year in revenue, but 97% of that revenue comes from oil and gas. The Land Office must diversify its revenue, with more renewable projects (wind, solar, geothermal), commercial projects (on appropriate parcels), and housing projects (again, on appropriate parcels).

Would you support or oppose efforts to expand renewable energy installations on state trust land?

Absolutely support. It's one of the ways to diversify the revenue of the Land Office, and it builds towards the coming energy transition away from fossil fuels. New Mexico is an energy producing state, and with its substantial wind, solar, and geothermal resources, it will be in the future.

Given the legacy of Jeffrey Epstein's Zorro Ranch in New Mexico, would you propose any changes to how the State Land Office handles and regulates applications for grazing leases?

The State Land Office has already started requiring additional information from potential lessees about the actual, beneficial ownersnot merely anonymous, closely-held corporations or LLCs. I will draw on my professional background as an attorney and build on that practice.

Do you support or oppose the state's current oil and gas royalty rate structure? If elected, would you seek to make any changes to the current royalty rates?

In the Legislature I worked to increase the maximum royalty rate from 20% to 25% (Senate Bill 23, 2025). This rate better reflects fair market value and will increase payments to the Land Grant Permanent Fund by $1 Billion over the life of the bill. I support this rate structure.

What would your approach to potential land swaps be? In addition, what criteria would you use to evaluate such transactions?

Land swaps are an important tool to deal with New Mexico's checkerboarded and fragmented landscape. Land swaps must, of course, be a net benefit to the state, not sacrifice public access for outdoor recreation (including hunting and fishing), and have a robust public review and input process.

Have you or your business, if you are a business owner, ever been the subject of any state or federal tax liens?

Yes. In 2022 I failed to file the proper form with the Department of Workforce Solutions and ended up with a $172 business lien. (It was quickly paid.)

Have you ever been arrested for, charged with, or convicted of drunken driving, any misdemeanor or any felony in New Mexico or any other state? If so, explain.

No.

Have you ever been involved in a personal or business bankruptcy proceeding?

No.

Challengers

Land Commissioner: Jonas Moya (D)

Land Commissioner: Juan Sanchez (D)

urn:contentItem:6JJC-BS63-RX5X-H41Y-00000-00Our View: Picks: Haaland, Hull for gov., Luján for U.S. Senate2026-05-10T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:01:08ZLexisNexisBPg. 2Our View: Picks: Haaland, Hull for gov., Luján for U.S. SenateThe New Mexican

Governor: Democratic primaryThe Democratic primary race between Bernalillo County District Attorney Sam Bregman and former U.S. Rep. and Department of the Interior Secretary Deb Haaland features tough talk vs. soft speech.

It goes without saying that Haaland is a historic candidate - if elected, she could become the first Native female governor in the United States. But that's not why Democrats should choose her as their nominee.

The Laguna Pueblo tribal member would bring to the office her experience as interior secretary, where she supervised more than 70,000 employees and a budget of some $18 billion annually. She was responsible for protecting an estimated 1 million acres of land and water, advancing renewable energy projects and implementing the Federal Indian Boarding School initiative so the country can face its past.

Haaland has superior executive experience, crucial for a governor in charge of the executive branch. For doubters, there's one direct comparison. Haaland and Bregman both served as chairs of the state Democratic Party. He left the party in debt. She did not.

The two candidates have different views of how to move New Mexico beyond dependence on the oil and gas industry. In fact, Bregman held a fundraiser in 2025 at the Petroleum Club of Midland. For a Maryland-born, cowboy hat-wearing lawman, the affinity with Texas makes sense. However, this is about the best choice for governor of New Mexico. Haaland has taken stronger positions against reuse of produced water, in favor of renewable energy and for protection of Chaco Canyon from drilling.

We like that Haaland wants to address healthcare, providing coverage for more people in a state small enough to experiment with universal care. She is outspoken about the need to keep private equity out of utility management.

Beyond policy differences, Haaland is a collaborative leader, one who will listen to her Cabinet and other state employees. Most of all, she has spent her year of campaigning crisscrossing the state, meeting one-on-one and in small groups with tens of thousands of New Mexicans. She listens more than she speaks, giving her the on-the-ground knowledge she will need to tackle New Mexico problems. In the Democratic primary, The New Mexican endorses Deb Haaland.

Governor: Republican primary

This primary is wide open, with the sitting Rio Rancho mayor facing two business leaders with 40% of voters undecided. Anyone can win.

We have enjoyed talking to all the candidates who, at least in face-to-face conversations, avoid culture war issues and talk about specific policy solutions they have for New Mexico.

Duke Rodriguez, the cannabis CEO who doesn't believe Gov. Michelle Lujan Grisham's childcare initiative is legal, is open to innovations that would place New Mexico in a better competitive situation. Businessman Doug Turner brings a thoughtful, reasoned approach to politics. Both Rodriguez and Turner came out of the Gary Johnson administration - whether working inside it as Rodriguez did, or directing a campaign as Turner did.

Quieter, but more experienced in actual governing is Gregg Hull, 12-year mayor of Rio Rancho, a successful, welcoming city. For 12 of the years that the city has been growing and thriving, Hull was the mayor.

He can spend 30 minutes discussing the ins and outs of the gross receipts tax system and how he would reform it. Hull believes government needs to streamline itself to deliver basic services - whether for kids in schools, tax relief for business owners or building infrastructure improvements efficiently.

We can't help but like that in an earlier incarnation, Hull worked delivering The Santa Fe New Mexican, even completing his morning paper route before taking his wife to the hospital where she delivered their first child. His vision is of a New Mexico where adult children return home to raise their own children, bringing joy to grandparents across the state. In the GOP primary, The New Mexican endorses Gregg Hull.

In addition to the Republican and Democratic nominees, independent Ken Miyagishima will be on the November ballot as a candidate for governor.

U.S. Senate

Sen. Ben Ray Luján will be reelected, a result we soundly endorse. But we also want to thank Air Force veteran and democratic socialist Matt Dodson, who worked overtime to gather the signatures necessary to be on the ballot. Dodson represents the left wing of the Democratic Party, pushing more mainstream Democrats to stop taking corporate PAC dollars, lessen their support for Israel and focus on putting people before big donors. His participation makes the primary a contest - and Luján, as well as voters, should be grateful.

Having an opponent offers a platform for Luján to present his solid record of accomplishment, from expanding federal compensation for survivors of radiation exposure, to introducing the Epstein Transparency Act seeking justice and accountability, to supporting protection of public lands. Despite partisan differences, Luján successfully lobbied to raise the cap on the Navajo-Gallup Water project and to cap the costs for the city of Gallup - winning $120 million to fund the project and a $76 million cap for Gallup. He is working to protect public lands from invasive mining, quickly responding to potential uranium mining that would harm the Rio Chama region.

This is a senator who participates in the big fights while never forgetting the needs of his state. In the 2026 Democratic primary, The New Mexican endorses Ben Ray Luján.

GOP write-in candidate Larry E. Marker could make the general election ballot if he receives enough write-in votes, some 2,351 for this race.

Primary endorsements

Friday: County Commission, sheriff and state legislative races

Saturday: Lieutenant governor, secretary of state and state land commissioner

Today: Governor and U.S. Senate

ACTIVITIES:

Swinerton Incorporated provides commercial construction and construction management services in the Western United States. It offers general construction, modernization and renovation, management and consulting, preconstruction, corporate, and renewable energy services. The companys projects include aviation, cultural, education, federal, gaming and entertainment, healthcare, hospitality, interiors, mission critical, mixed-use/residential, office, renewable energy, retail, and special. Swinerton Incorporated was formerly known as Swinerton & Walberg Co. The company was founded in 1888 and is based in San Francisco, California with additional offices in California, Colorado, Hawaii, Texas, New Mexico, and Washington.

+ SWINERTON INCORPORATED [3 News That Matters]

+ SWINERTON STRENGTHENS MISSION CRITICAL TEAM WITH KRISTEN BEJARANO APPOINTMENT

Swinerton Incorporated has announced the appointment of Kristen Bejarano as Director of Mission Critical. This strategic move, revealed on the 8th of April, 2026, underscores Swinerton's commitment to enhancing its capabilities in the burgeoning mission critical and data center infrastructure market.Bejarano brings nearly 25 years of extensive experience in delivering complex, high-demand projects, having made significant contributions to the mission critical construction landscape for some of the largest global technology firms. Her notable achievements include launching and scaling a national mission critical and data center construction division, and managing the strategy and procurement for a major technology company's Americas data center program.In her new role at Swinerton, Bejarano will spearhead the company's mission critical strategy, collaborating with clients to translate intricate technical requirements into actionable plans that emphasize speed-to-market, sustainability, and energy efficiency. Chris Chany, Chief Revenue Officer of Swinerton, expressed confidence in Bejarano's ability to significantly enhance the growth of the mission critical sector while also improving client experiences.Bejarano's passion for the evolving landscape of mission critical engineering, particularly in areas such as cooling technologies and renewable energy integration, positions her as a key leader in delivering innovative solutions. Her leadership is expected to enable Swinerton to address the growing demands of the market while maintaining a strong focus on sustainability and operational efficiency.Swinerton Incorporated, founded in 1888 and wholly employee-owned, comprises Swinerton Builders, Timberlab, Inc., SAK Builders, Inc., and Lindgren Development Inc. It provides a range of construction services across the United States, with a commitment to quality and innovation in every project.

+ SWINERTON STRENGTHENS NORTHEAST DIVISION WITH NEW LEADERSHIP

Swinerton Builders, an industry-leading commercial general contractor with offices nationwide, has announced a significant leadership transition in its Northeast Division. This promotion of Jay Quackenbush to vice president and division manager is part of a broader strategy to bolster the firm’s presence in the growing Northeast construction market. Following this move, which took effect on 16th March 2026, the Northeast Division has also welcomed Nicholas Schifferle as director of client relations and Robert Murphy as project executive.Jay Quackenbush, a long-time New York native, brings nearly three decades of experience overseeing complex construction projects to his new role. His deep understanding of New York City's unique building conditions and extensive industry relationships position him well to drive the division's strategic goals. Ray Haj, Swinerton's chief operational officer and East Coast regional manager, emphasized that Quackenbush's leadership will enhance the team's capacity to meet client needs, ranging from facility management to large-scale ground-up projects. Furthermore, Quackenbush is actively involved in community engagement, serving on several boards and partnering with local nonprofits.Nicholas Schifferle, who joined Swinerton in 2025, adds more than 20 years of international business development experience to the team. His background includes managing high-profile design and construction projects across Asia and Australia, as well as founding a design-build firm in San Francisco. Quackenbush noted Schifferle's ability to adapt to rapid industry changes and his people-focused approach, which has made him a valuable asset to both the team and its clients. Schifferle's longstanding involvement with CoreNet Global reflects his commitment to advancing the commercial real estate sector.Meanwhile, Robert Murphy, with his extensive portfolio of overseeing over $650 million in commercial interiors construction, is tasked with leading multiple project teams to ensure quality and client satisfaction. Quackenbush praised Murphy's operational capabilities and his ability to elevate team performance. Together with long-standing division leaders Sloane Brown and Peter Fontana, Murphy will work to grow Swinerton’s footprint in the Northeast's commercial interiors market.Since its establishment in the Northeast in 2021, Swinerton's division has delivered a variety of significant projects, including office fit-outs, historic renovations, and ground-up constructions across all five boroughs. With a robust project pipeline and a commitment to excellence, the team continues to expand its portfolio into education, residential, and public sectors. Quackenbush affirmed that the division is well-positioned for future growth, driven by a talented team dedicated to serving the communities in which they operate.

+ SWINERTON ANNOUNCES LEADERSHIP TRANSITION AS DON ADAIR RETIRES

Swinerton Incorporated has announced that Executive Vice President and Chief Revenue Officer (CRO) Don Adair will retire on January 9, 2026, after nearly four decades in the construction industry. This leadership change comes as the company prepares for continued growth and strategic development under new leadership.Adair's retirement was officially communicated to the employee-owners of Swinerton on 13th June 2025, marking the conclusion of a notable career that began in 1988 when he joined Swinerton Builders as a Project Engineer in Los Angeles. Throughout his tenure, Adair held various pivotal roles, including expanding Swinerton Builders' San Diego office and ultimately being named Executive Vice President and CRO in 2019.In his retirement announcement, Adair expressed pride in the company's culture, emphasizing the importance of building strong teams and lasting relationships. 'Watching those efforts grow into long-term success for both our teams and our partners has been one of the most rewarding parts of my journey,' he stated.Chris Chany, who has collaborated with Adair for nearly a decade, has been appointed as his successor. Chany joined Swinerton Builders in 2013 and has since become known for his ability to foster partnerships and drive strategic growth. His focus on delivering extraordinary client experiences and reliable project execution positions him as a strong candidate to continue the momentum established by Adair.In his new role, Chany emphasized a commitment to maintaining the company's core values while pursuing new opportunities for growth. 'I am honored to step into this role and continue building on Don's framework,' Chany remarked. 'Our focus will remain on building strong relationships and driving growth for our teams and clients.' With Chany at the helm, Swinerton aims to enhance its reputation as a leading player in the construction sector.

+ MAINMARK UK JOINS BALFOUR BEATTY'S CP7 FRAMEWORK FOR RAIL INFRASTRUCTURE

Ground engineering specialist Mainmark UK has secured a place on the Balfour Beatty plc CP7 Framework, positioning the company to support the delivery of key rail infrastructure works across Network Rail’s latest five-year investment programme. This investment period, known as Control Period 7 (CP7), spans from 1st April 2024 to 31st March 2029, with an allocation of approximately £43–45 billion across Great Britain.The CP7 programme focuses on maintaining, renewing, and modernising the rail network to deliver a safer, more reliable, and more sustainable railway. Investment priorities during CP7 include infrastructure maintenance and renewals, digital signalling and technology, safety improvements, and initiatives aimed at enhancing both freight and passenger performance across the network.As part of the CP7 Framework, contractors will engage in a variety of works aligned with Network Rail’s Strategic Business Plan. These activities will encompass low-complexity emergency callouts, planned preventative maintenance, and small-scale civil engineering schemes. Mainmark UK will be involved in geotechnical response works, bridge repairs, structural integrity projects, platform refurbishments, and drainage clearance, among others.Tom Kavanagh, General Manager at Mainmark UK, expressed enthusiasm about the collaboration with Balfour Beatty, stating, “We’re looking forward to collaborating with Balfour Beatty to help deliver critical infrastructure works across the network during the CP7 period. Being selected to support Balfour Beatty on this framework is a fantastic opportunity for our team and aligns perfectly with our growth plans.”Mainmark is known for its innovative ground engineering solutions, particularly its Teretek® resin injection technology, which stabilises weak ground conditions and fills subsurface voids without extensive excavation. This technology is crucial for maintaining the integrity of rail infrastructure, especially as heavier freight loads and severe weather events put increasing pressure on railway assets. With its appointment to the CP7 Framework, Mainmark UK is set to play a vital role in ensuring the resilience and reliability of the UK's rail network.

+ BALFOUR BEATTY'S GROWTH FUELED BY RECORD ORDER BOOK

Balfour Beatty PLC has reported impressive growth, driven by its robust performance in the construction sector, particularly in power transmission projects. The company’s market valuation has soared nearly two-thirds over the past year, a noteworthy achievement for a FTSE 250 constituent. Operating profits within Balfour Beatty's support services segment saw a remarkable increase of one-third, amounting to £122 million, alongside an 80 basis point rise in the underlying margin to 8.5 percent. The company’s UK construction division achieved its margin targets ahead of schedule, underscoring its operational efficiency.In the US market, Balfour Beatty has capitalized on rising building volumes although it faced challenges due to cost overruns in a project within its Civils arm. The dual market strategy appears to be paying off, as the company navigates through these hurdles while maintaining a focus on profitability. The expansion of infrastructure projects, particularly in power transmission, has provided a solid foundation for growth, enabling Balfour Beatty to solidify its position in the competitive construction landscape.Market analysts remain optimistic about Balfour Beatty’s future prospects, considering its substantial order book which continues to grow amidst a backdrop of increasing infrastructure investment. The company’s strategic initiatives to enhance operational margins and deliver projects on time have positioned it well for sustained growth in the coming quarters. Investors are likely to monitor Balfour Beatty’s performance closely as it seeks to leverage its strengths in both the UK and US markets.As Balfour Beatty continues to capitalize on the opportunities presented by the expanding power grid and overall construction demand, it remains crucial for stakeholders to assess the potential impacts of rising costs and project management challenges. Overall, the company’s recent performance indicates a strong recovery trajectory, making it a noteworthy player in the construction sector as it heads into the next fiscal year.

+ HENSEL PHELPS CONSTRUCTION CO [3 News That Matters]

+ TRACK3D SECURES MAJOR PARTNERSHIP WITH HENSEL PHELPS FOR CONSTRUCTION TECHNOLOGY

Track3D, an AI-driven construction technology company, has marked a significant milestone by securing a multi-year agreement with Hensel Phelps, expanding its collaboration from a single pilot to the deployment of Track3D’s Reality Intelligence platform across more than 200 active projects. This announcement was made on 1st April 2026, emphasizing Track3D's growing influence in the construction sector.Hensel Phelps plans to standardize the use of Track3D for portfolio-wide progress monitoring, indicating strong confidence in the platform’s scalability and effectiveness across diverse construction projects. By utilizing automated progress tracking, Hensel Phelps aims to identify on-site issues earlier, which can significantly reduce delays and enhance schedule reliability, especially in complex builds.A notable case study from San Francisco International Airport highlighted the successful application of Track3D’s technology on a challenging infrastructure project. Project leaders reported a shift from reactive issue resolution to proactive decision-making, facilitated by 360deg site walks and the involvement of existing field personnel, thereby minimizing the need for major workflow changes. This deployment at SFO reinforces Track3D's relevance in high-value infrastructure work and solidifies its position in the reality intelligence market.In addition to the partnership with Hensel Phelps, Track3D has introduced a March update to its platform that focuses on large-enterprise oversight and workflow efficiency. Key enhancements include an admin dashboard and Groups functionality, which are designed to provide major contractors with centralized portfolio visibility and streamlined user management. Furthermore, the new capture-free progress tracking feature allows teams to document job site status directly on floorplans, promoting continuous oversight.Track3D is also intensifying its go-to-market strategy by targeting specialty contractors, with plans to participate in the AWCI Convention & Expo 2026. The company aims to showcase tools that automate production tracking and reduce manual reporting for drywall and ceiling professionals. Collectively, these developments—ranging from the Hensel Phelps portfolio rollout to enterprise-focused product enhancements—underscore Track3D’s strengthening competitive position in construction technology and may pave the way for sustainable growth in the future.

+ BARTON MALOW COMPANY [3 News That Matters]

+ BARTON MALOW TO LEAD CONSTRUCTION OF ALUMNIFI FIELD FOR DETROIT CITY FC

Southfield-based Barton Malow has been selected as the construction manager for the Detroit City Football Club’s (Detroit City FC) new home stadium, AlumniFi Field. This announcement comes as the project is set to transform the site of the former Southwest Detroit Hospital into a 15,000-seat venue, with a projected opening date in spring 2027.The construction manager role was confirmed on 28th April 2026, as Detroit City FC continues to expand its presence in the USL Championship. With fan attendance at its current 7,000-seat home, Keyworth Stadium, regularly exceeding capacity, the demand for a larger facility has never been more pressing. AlumniFi Field will be strategically located between Detroit’s Corktown and Mexicantown neighborhoods, aiming to create a vibrant community hub.Designed to capture the essence of traditional European football stadiums, AlumniFi Field will feature a natural grass pitch, three permanent grandstands equipped with canopies, and rooms for various community activities. The venue is intended to host not only soccer matches but also concerts and other events, reinforcing its role as a gathering place for local residents.In collaboration with Detroit City FC, Barton Malow plans to honor the legacy of the former hospital, which was recognized for its commitment to diversity in hiring practices when it opened in 1973. Sean Hollister, senior vice president of Barton Malow, emphasized that this project aligns with the company's mission of technical excellence and community commitment.Sean Mann, CEO of Detroit City FC, echoed this sentiment, stating that AlumniFi Field will reflect the character and authenticity of Detroit. Both leaders expressed a shared vision for the stadium as a space that fosters community engagement and is shaped by the passions of local supporters.

+ BARTON MALOW TO LEAD WEST TOWER PROJECT AT MILAN PUSKAR STADIUM

MORGANTOWN, W.Va. - West Virginia University's Department of Intercollegiate Athletics will partner with Barton Malow and March-Westin to serve as construction managers on the Milan Puskar Stadium West Tower Project, which is set to begin after the 2026 football season. The joint venture brings together a power team of national and local construction leadership to the project.As the construction managers, Barton Malow and March-Westin will oversee all construction from conception to completion, ensuring that the project is finished safely, on time and within budget. By managing the daily on-site operations, the team will be the primary liaison between the university, architects and engineers, handling preconstruction planning, cost and schedule management, procurement, and coordination of onsite construction activities."We have experienced how special game day is at Milan Puskar Stadium, and we are excited to be a part of this partnership with the university on this renovation," said Scott Mull, Regional Executive at Barton Malow. "Our team is ready to bring the same focus and craftsmanship we've delivered on athletic facilities across the country to help create something Mountaineer fans will be proud of for generations."The joint venture combines complementary expertise in the unique campus and stadium environments. March-Westin brings decades of experience delivering work at Milan Puskar Stadium and across the university's two main campuses, while Barton Malow contributes extensive experience leading collegiate and professional stadium renovations nationwide."Our team is proud to partner with the university's Athletics, Legends Global as the owner's representative, the SLAM Collaborative design team, and Barton Malow in this joint venture to deliver a transformational renovation in Milan Puskar Stadium," said March-Westin President Jamie Ridgeway. "March-Westin's local knowledge combined with Barton Malow's national experience uniquely positions our team to successfully complete this project with minimal disruption to the fan experience."

+ BARTON MALOW PARTNERS WITH SHERIDAN FOR UPCOMING MACON ARENA INFORMATION SESSION

Barton Malow and Sheridan Construction will host an information session for subcontractors and suppliers regarding the New Macon Arena project. This session aims to introduce the project scope, schedule, and available trade bid opportunities to interested firms and companies, including those classified as SLBE, MBE, WBE, and DBE. The event is scheduled for 24th March 2026, from 11:00 a.m. to 12:30 p.m. at the Macon Centreplex, located at 200 Coliseum Drive.The session will feature a short presentation followed by a Q&A segment, allowing attendees to engage with project leads and network effectively. Although registration is not mandatory, it is encouraged for participants to register ahead of time through the Barton Malow website. This initiative aims to foster connections with local businesses and provide them with insights into the project, which is expected to significantly impact the community's economic landscape.Mayor Lester Miller commented on the significance of the new arena, stating, "This arena won’t be just a venue; it will be a destination and major economic engine that creates opportunities, attracts visitors, and keeps our city competitive for years to come." The development is part of a broader effort to enhance the entertainment and event infrastructure in the region, complementing other facilities like the Atrium Health Amphitheater and the recently renovated Macon City Auditorium.The Office of Small Business Affairs (OSBA) in Macon-Bibb County supports this outreach event, encouraging local vendors to participate. OSBA Director Charise Stephens-Merriweather emphasized the importance of involving local, small, and minority businesses in this substantial project, stating, "The size of a project does not come along very often, and we want to make sure our local businesses have a chance to make a competitive bid and be involved from the very beginning."The new Macon Arena will be constructed on the current Macon Coliseum site and is anticipated to cover over 230,000 square feet, with a seating capacity of approximately 8,500 for concerts. It will feature modern amenities and a flexible design suitable for various events, including sports. FREDERICK, MD — Turner Construction Company celebrated the topping out of the Aligned IAD-06 Data Center in Frederick, Maryland, marking the completion of the structural phase for the 72-megawatt facility. This significant milestone was achieved on the 28th of April 2026, less than five months after breaking ground, showcasing the effectiveness of early planning and strong collaboration among the project team.To date, the project has seen over 80,000 craft hours logged, with approximately 345 skilled workers contributing to its progress. "Topping out this facility represents more than completing the structural frame," stated Nick Sevier, Operations Manager at Turner Construction Company. The facility features a two-story structural steel design, an insulated precast exterior system, and a high-efficiency hybrid cooling solution that combines both liquid and air-cooled technologies to optimize operational performance.The successful topping out of the IAD-06 Data Center underscores Turner Construction's expertise in managing complex projects in the data center sector, which is rapidly evolving to accommodate the surge in digital services. + TURNER CONSTRUCTION COMMENCES VERTICAL BUILD ON $900M GEISINGER EXPANSION

WILKES-BARRE, PA — Turner Construction has begun vertical construction on the $900 million expansion of Geisinger Wyoming Valley Medical Center, marking a major milestone in one of northeastern Pennsylvania’s largest health care investments. The date of this announcement is 28th April 2026.After years of planning and foundational work, steel is now rising on the 11-story, 600,000-square-foot patient tower. This new structure will increase the hospital's capacity from 356 to 500 beds and expand the number of operating rooms from 12 to 18. Additionally, the emergency department will be enlarged, supporting Geisinger’s goal of providing all private patient rooms. The facility is expected to open in the first quarter of 2028.David Kaminski, Vice President & Senior General Manager at Turner Construction, stated, “This milestone marks an important step forward in delivering expanded, modern health care access for families across northeastern Pennsylvania. We are proud to partner with Geisinger on a transformational project that will enhance patient care, support caregivers, and strengthen this community for decades to come.”The project is anticipated to support up to 3,800 construction jobs and generate approximately $1 billion in local economic impact. The onsite workforce is expected to grow from 150 to as many as 450 workers during peak activity. Upon completion, Geisinger plans to hire an additional 400 employees, further contributing to local job creation.Originally opened in 1981, the Geisinger Wyoming Valley campus has expanded over the decades to meet the growing demand for health services. This latest addition will bring advanced medical capabilities comparable to those found in major academic medical centers located in large metropolitan areas.

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION SELECTED FOR AMBITIOUS WESTCOURT ORLANDO PROJECT

Turner Construction has been named the general contractor for the Westcourt Orlando mixed-use project, an ambitious development planned for West Church Street in downtown Orlando. The announcement was made by developers on the 22nd of April, 2026, marking a significant milestone in the project’s progression.The decision to partner with Turner Construction stems from a collaborative approach taken by JMA Ventures, the project developer. Todd Chapman, president of JMA Ventures, noted that Turner’s team demonstrated exceptional capabilities in prior complex builds, especially along Florida’s Space Coast, which includes coordinating infrastructure for rocket launches. Chapman recalled an early meeting where he expressed confidence in their ability to tackle the Westcourt project, saying, “If you guys can send things into space, I’m pretty sure you can work with us.”Turner Construction has been involved in the Westcourt project since 2022, focusing on pre-construction activities alongside JMA Ventures and Machete Group. Jeff Justen, vice president and general manager of Turner’s Orlando Business Unit, indicated that the early site activities have commenced, including utility mapping and surveying. These preparatory measures are critical for ensuring a smooth transition into the next phases of construction.The Westcourt project has faced some delays, shifting timelines due to financing challenges and fluctuations in construction costs. However, Chapman emphasized that involving Turner Construction early in the process allowed for valuable input on design and execution strategies. “We don’t want to design in a vacuum,” he said, highlighting the importance of contractor collaboration during the planning stages.Valued at approximately $500 million, the Westcourt development aims to create a vibrant mixed-use space featuring a hotel, residential units, office space, retail, and a live entertainment venue adjacent to the Kia Center. During peak construction, the project is expected to support around 800 workers on-site, contributing to the local job market in Central Florida. Chapman expressed excitement over the project’s complexity, stating that JMA Ventures is drawn to intricate developments, which informed their choice in partners like Turner Construction.

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION TACKLES COLORADO RIVER CRISIS WITH INNOVATIVE WATER SOLUTIONS

As the Colorado River faces unprecedented challenges, PCL Construction Enterprises, Inc. is stepping up to provide innovative solutions aimed at water resilience. On the 26th of March 2026, the urgency surrounding the Colorado River's dwindling water supply became a focal point for communities across the Western United States, as they seek alternatives to secure their water futures.The Colorado River serves as a vital resource for millions, yet it is currently grappling with severe drought conditions. Lake Powell, for instance, is at an alarming 162 feet below full capacity, holding only 26% of its total water volume. The U.S. Bureau of Reclamation warns that if current conditions persist, Lake Mead could reach record lows within two years, prompting a critical reassessment of water management strategies across the basin states.PCL Construction is at the forefront of this reassessment, advocating for investments in water reuse infrastructure as a key strategy for bolstering water security. With projected U.S. investments in water reuse expected to average $47.1 billion over the next decade, PCL is positioned to support municipalities in capturing and reusing water to mitigate the effects of ongoing drought. Although adoption rates vary significantly among the seven Colorado River basin states, PCL's initiatives exemplify how infrastructure investments can enhance resilience in water supply systems.The urgency of the situation is underscored by the imminent expiration of current water management rules in the fall of 2026. As states grapple with the looming deadline to ratify Colorado River water allocation agreements, PCL Construction's projects, such as the Drought Pipeline Program in Phoenix, provide tangible examples of how communities can adapt to a drier future. This program has been crucial in ensuring the reliability of water supply for the rapidly growing Phoenix area, which has seen a 12% population increase over the last decade.Industry leaders, including PCL, recently gathered at the Colorado River Water Users Association Conference to discuss collaborative strategies for securing the river's future. The consensus was clear: a multifaceted approach is necessary to address the complexities of climate change and aging infrastructure. PCL Construction's commitment to advancing water reuse initiatives positions the company as a critical player in shaping sustainable water management practices that can withstand the challenges posed by the Colorado River's ongoing crisis.

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION ADVANCES $192M AI-READY DATA CENTER PROJECT

PCL Construction Services, Inc. and PCL Industrial Services, Inc., operating companies of PCL Construction, have reached structural completion on a $192 million, 22.5-megawatt data center, advancing one of the largest digital infrastructure projects currently underway in the Denver metropolitan area. On 19th March 2026, the companies celebrated this significant milestone that underscores their commitment to delivering state-of-the-art facilities designed for high-capacity digital workloads.Located on a 17-acre site southeast of Denver, the project is being delivered by PCL’s Minneapolis Buildings group in collaboration with the Denver team, combining longstanding client relationships with local market expertise. The data center is specifically designed to support enterprise-scale, compute-intensive workloads, including artificial intelligence and graphics processing unit applications, as demand for high-capacity digital infrastructure continues to grow across the region.PCL’s scope of work includes the phased construction of five steel-frame colocation data halls totaling approximately 277,000 square feet, allowing for efficient onboarding of new tenants while maintaining flexibility for future expansion. The project also features a dedicated administration building with office space for personnel and on-site operations teams. This design aims to accommodate high-density deployments, aligning performance needs with modern sustainability and energy-efficiency requirements.The project includes comprehensive electrical infrastructure to support continuous, high-performance operations, from permanent and temporary power systems to sitewide distribution, security, and communications pathways. Key work includes installing and integrating major power and cooling equipment that enables reliable, high-density data center operations. PCL Industrial Services, Inc. is self-performing the core electrical work while coordinating specialized partners for testing and life-safety systems.PCL Construction’s extensive experience in delivering mission-critical and data center facilities across the United States is critical on complex, high-density projects like this, according to Ryan Schmidt, vice president and district manager of Denver Buildings. The project teams recently marked the topping-out ceremony, celebrating the dedication, craftsmanship, and collaboration that will transition the development into interior build-out and systems installation, with construction scheduled for completion in summer 2027.

JE Dunn Construction has launched its new off-site manufacturing initiative, Form Off-Site Solutions(TradeMark), designed to specialize in Design for Manufacturing (DfM) for complex construction components. KANSAS CITY, Mo. — Construction has reached a key milestone on the Northland Workforce Development Center, as JE Dunn Construction and project partners recently celebrated the topping out of the new facility. + AUDDIA AND THRAMANN MERGE TO ESTABLISH MCCARTHY FINNEY HOLDING

Auddia Inc. has announced a definitive merger agreement with Thramann Holdings to form a new Delaware holding company named McCarthy Finney, Inc. The announcement was made on 17th February 2026, and the details of the merger were disclosed on 18th February 2026. Under this agreement, both Auddia and Thramann will become wholly owned subsidiaries of McCarthy Finney, with Auddia shareholders receiving one common share of McCarthy Finney for each share they hold in Auddia, while Thramann owners will receive a combination of special preferred stock and $3.5 million in unsecured notes.Post-merger, Auddia shareholders are projected to own approximately 20% of McCarthy Finney, while former Thramann stakeholders are expected to hold around 80%, contingent on Auddia maintaining a minimum cash balance of $12 million. The special preferred stock issued to Thramann will carry significant governance rights, including a liquidation preference of at least $20.5 million, alongside flexible conversion and redemption features, giving Thramann substantial influence over the newly formed entity.Both Auddia and Thramann's boards have unanimously approved the merger, with Auddia's independent special committee affirming the deal's fairness, supported by a fairness opinion from Houlihan Capital. Governance continuity is assured, with Auddia's four directors set to join the McCarthy Finney board, while Jeff Thramann and John Mahoney will continue as CEO and CFO, respectively. Additionally, Auddia's executives and directors have committed to support the deal, and Thramann will adhere to a 180-day lock-up on his shares in McCarthy Finney.In terms of valuation, Auddia estimates a base-case worth of $250 million for McCarthy Finney, based on a management-prepared discounted cash flow analysis of the combined entity's pro forma over the next decade. The merger is anticipated to close in the second quarter of 2026, pending Auddia shareholder approval, SEC effectiveness of a Form S-4 filing, and meeting Nasdaq listing requirements. The formation of McCarthy Finney positions the company as a diversified holding entity focused on artificial intelligence applications across distributed AI data centers, healthtech value-based care, and AI-driven travel services.Auddia, based in Boulder, Colorado, is recognized for its innovative audio technology that leverages a proprietary AI platform to enhance consumer engagement with AM/FM radio, podcasts, and other audio content. Its flagship product, the 'faidr' superapp, aims to modernize music consumption and promotion by offering ad-free listening, content and ad-break skipping, and integrated artist discovery.

+ MCCARTHY HOLDINGS ADVANCES SUSTAINABILITY WITH 2024 GHG EMISSIONS REPORT

McCarthy Holdings, one of the largest employee-owned construction firms in the U.S., has released its updated 2024 Greenhouse Gas (GHG) Emissions Report. The report was published on 16th February 2026, and reflects McCarthy's commitment to strengthening its sustainability efforts across its operations and subsidiaries.Developed in partnership with Gravity, a leading enterprise carbon accounting and energy management platform, the report provides a comprehensive view of McCarthy's carbon footprint. It covers Scope 1, Scope 2, and key categories of Scope 3 emissions, with Scopes 1 and 2 having undergone independent auditing and awarded limited assurance. This commitment to transparency and accountability aligns with McCarthy's mission to be the best builder in America.Ian Pope, Senior Director of Sustainability at McCarthy Holdings, emphasized the company's dedication to community building. 'As an employee-owned company, this important effort is in line with our core values and provides our teams with the practical data necessary to meet regulatory requirements,' he stated. McCarthy's sustainability strategy is anchored in three primary priorities: understanding its environmental impact, acting responsibly for its people and communities, and efficiently managing energy consumption.In addition to emissions reporting, McCarthy Holdings integrates responsible practices into its projects. This includes biodiversity initiatives, responsible fuel management, and the use of digital tools and artificial intelligence for smarter project decisions. The company is also focusing on responsible end-of-life management for solar panels through its advanced PV recycling program, partnering with Solar Energy Industries Association (SEIA)-approved recyclers to divert waste from landfills and support a circular economy.By leveraging emissions data and operational insights, McCarthy Holdings continues to demonstrate that its role as a builder of communities extends to fostering a more sustainable future. With a commitment to environmental stewardship and community well-being, McCarthy is setting benchmarks in the construction industry for responsible practices.

value.Title: State Land Office approves 212-megawatt wind farm for Torrance County

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHN-9493-S00R-T0XW-00000-00State Land Office approves 212-megawatt wind farm for Torrance County2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:01:08ZLexisNexisState Land Office approves 212-megawatt wind farm for Torrance CountyMatthew Reichbach

The State Land Office approved a new 10,160-acre, 212-megawatt wind farm for Torrance County, enough to power more than 60,000 homes.

State Land Commissioner Stephanie Garcia Richard announced the project May 6 for the county east of the Sandia Mountains.

The State Land Office signed an agreement with AFE Cedarvale Wind LLC, a subsidiary of MMR Group/Southwestern Power Group, for the project.

"We keep powering ahead with deals that are making New Mexico a leader in renewable energy development," Garcia Richard said. "Before I came into office, there wasn't a comprehensive, strategic approach to developing renewable energy on state lands. That has all changed under my watch, and renewable energy companies now know the State Land Office is the place to do business. Projects like this one are job creators in our rural communities and create long-lasting revenue streams for our public schools and other institutions."

Garcia Richard has pushed renewable energy on state trust lands. Her second term ends at the end of this year. State elected officials are limited to two consecutive terms.

According to the State Land Office, renewable energy production on state trust land has increased from about 400 megawatts to about 3,271 megawatts during Garcia Richard's tenure.

For projects on state trust land, companies must honor all terms of the lease throughout the project's lifespan, including pre-construction. AFE Cedarvale Wind LLC will be required to provide a project plan to the State Land Office six months before construction and file annual reports throughout construction.

Renewable energy is central to New Mexico's energy policy. State law says that investor-owned utilities must generate 100% of their energy using renewable sources by 2045. Rural co-ops have until 2050.

The SunZia wind project in southeastern New Mexico, years in the making, began generating electricity. That project sends energy to California, another state with an ambitious renewable energy goal. SunZia is projected to generate 3,500 megawatts.

The State Land Office has generated $14 billion since 2019.

value.Title: SWINERTON INCORPORATED - THE RECENT HEADLINES WITH PEER NEWS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH5-B993-S4F4-J51G-00000-00SWINERTON INCORPORATED - THE RECENT HEADLINES WITH PEER NEWS2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:01:08ZLexisNexisSTOCKSWINERTON INCORPORATED - THE RECENT HEADLINES WITH PEER NEWS

DASHBOARD: SWINERTON INCORPORATED

PermID4296594671Websitehttp://www.swinerton.comIndustryConstruction & EngineeringAddress5707 SOUTHWEST PARKWAY, BLDG. 1, SUITE 200 | AUSTIN, TX 78735

ACTIVITIES:

Swinerton Incorporated provides commercial construction and construction management services in the Western United States. It offers general construction, modernization and renovation, management and consulting, preconstruction, corporate, and renewable energy services. The companys projects include aviation, cultural, education, federal, gaming and entertainment, healthcare, hospitality, interiors, mission critical, mixed-use/residential, office, renewable energy, retail, and special. Swinerton Incorporated was formerly known as Swinerton & Walberg Co. The company was founded in 1888 and is based in San Francisco, California with additional offices in California, Colorado, Hawaii, Texas, New Mexico, and Washington.

+ SWINERTON INCORPORATED [3 News That Matters]

+ SWINERTON STRENGTHENS MISSION CRITICAL TEAM WITH KRISTEN BEJARANO APPOINTMENT

Swinerton Incorporated has announced the appointment of Kristen Bejarano as Director of Mission Critical. This strategic move, revealed on the 8th of April, 2026, underscores Swinerton's commitment to enhancing its capabilities in the burgeoning mission critical and data center infrastructure market.Bejarano brings nearly 25 years of extensive experience in delivering complex, high-demand projects, having made significant contributions to the mission critical construction landscape for some of the largest global technology firms. Her notable achievements include launching and scaling a national mission critical and data center construction division, and managing the strategy and procurement for a major technology company's Americas data center program.In her new role at Swinerton, Bejarano will spearhead the company's mission critical strategy, collaborating with clients to translate intricate technical requirements into actionable plans that emphasize speed-to-market, sustainability, and energy efficiency. Chris Chany, Chief Revenue Officer of Swinerton, expressed confidence in Bejarano's ability to significantly enhance the growth of the mission critical sector while also improving client experiences.Bejarano's passion for the evolving landscape of mission critical engineering, particularly in areas such as cooling technologies and renewable energy integration, positions her as a key leader in delivering innovative solutions. Her leadership is expected to enable Swinerton to address the growing demands of the market while maintaining a strong focus on sustainability and operational efficiency.Swinerton Incorporated, founded in 1888 and wholly employee-owned, comprises Swinerton Builders, Timberlab, Inc., SAK Builders, Inc., and Lindgren Development Inc. It provides a range of construction services across the United States, with a commitment to quality and innovation in every project.

+ SWINERTON STRENGTHENS NORTHEAST DIVISION WITH NEW LEADERSHIP

Swinerton Builders, an industry-leading commercial general contractor with offices nationwide, has announced a significant leadership transition in its Northeast Division. This promotion of Jay Quackenbush to vice president and division manager is part of a broader strategy to bolster the firm’s presence in the growing Northeast construction market. Following this move, which took effect on 16th March 2026, the Northeast Division has also welcomed Nicholas Schifferle as director of client relations and Robert Murphy as project executive.Jay Quackenbush, a long-time New York native, brings nearly three decades of experience overseeing complex construction projects to his new role. His deep understanding of New York City's unique building conditions and extensive industry relationships position him well to drive the division's strategic goals. The facility features a two-story structural steel design, an insulated precast exterior system, and a high-efficiency hybrid cooling solution that combines both liquid and air-cooled technologies to optimize operational performance.The successful topping out of the IAD-06 Data Center underscores Turner Construction's expertise in managing complex projects in the data center sector, which is rapidly evolving to accommodate the surge in digital services. The collaborative efforts demonstrated by Turner and its partners are pivotal in ensuring that the project meets its ambitious timeline and quality standards.As the project moves into the next phases of development, stakeholders will be closely monitoring the advancements made by Turner Construction and Aligned. With the structural phase completed, the focus will shift to the interior fit-out and operational readiness of the facility, further solidifying its role in enhancing the region's digital infrastructure.

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION COMMENCES VERTICAL BUILD ON $900M GEISINGER EXPANSION

WILKES-BARRE, PA — Turner Construction has begun vertical construction on the $900 million expansion of Geisinger Wyoming Valley Medical Center, marking a major milestone in one of northeastern Pennsylvania’s largest health care investments. The date of this announcement is 28th April 2026.After years of planning and foundational work, steel is now rising on the 11-story, 600,000-square-foot patient tower. This new structure will increase the hospital's capacity from 356 to 500 beds and expand the number of operating rooms from 12 to 18. Additionally, the emergency department will be enlarged, supporting Geisinger’s goal of providing all private patient rooms. The facility is expected to open in the first quarter of 2028.David Kaminski, Vice President & Senior General Manager at Turner Construction, stated, “This milestone marks an important step forward in delivering expanded, modern health care access for families across northeastern Pennsylvania. We are proud to partner with Geisinger on a transformational project that will enhance patient care, support caregivers, and strengthen this community for decades to come.”The project is anticipated to support up to 3,800 construction jobs and generate approximately $1 billion in local economic impact. The onsite workforce is expected to grow from 150 to as many as 450 workers during peak activity. Upon completion, Geisinger plans to hire an additional 400 employees, further contributing to local job creation.Originally opened in 1981, the Geisinger Wyoming Valley campus has expanded over the decades to meet the growing demand for health services. This latest addition will bring advanced medical capabilities comparable to those found in major academic medical centers located in large metropolitan areas.

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION SELECTED FOR AMBITIOUS WESTCOURT ORLANDO PROJECT

Turner Construction has been named the general contractor for the Westcourt Orlando mixed-use project, an ambitious development planned for West Church Street in downtown Orlando. The announcement was made by developers on the 22nd of April, 2026, marking a significant milestone in the project’s progression.The decision to partner with Turner Construction stems from a collaborative approach taken by JMA Ventures, the project developer. Todd Chapman, president of JMA Ventures, noted that Turner’s team demonstrated exceptional capabilities in prior complex builds, especially along Florida’s Space Coast, which includes coordinating infrastructure for rocket launches. Chapman recalled an early meeting where he expressed confidence in their ability to tackle the Westcourt project, saying, “If you guys can send things into space, I’m pretty sure you can work with us.”Turner Construction has been involved in the Westcourt project since 2022, focusing on pre-construction activities alongside JMA Ventures and Machete Group. Jeff Justen, vice president and general manager of Turner’s Orlando Business Unit, indicated that the early site activities have commenced, including utility mapping and surveying. These preparatory measures are critical for ensuring a smooth transition into the next phases of construction.The Westcourt project has faced some delays, shifting timelines due to financing challenges and fluctuations in construction costs. However, Chapman emphasized that involving Turner Construction early in the process allowed for valuable input on design and execution strategies. “We don’t want to design in a vacuum,” he said, highlighting the importance of contractor collaboration during the planning stages.Valued at approximately $500 million, the Westcourt development aims to create a vibrant mixed-use space featuring a hotel, residential units, office space, retail, and a live entertainment venue adjacent to the Kia Center. During peak construction, the project is expected to support around 800 workers on-site, contributing to the local job market in Central Florida. Chapman expressed excitement over the project’s complexity, stating that JMA Ventures is drawn to intricate developments, which informed their choice in partners like Turner Construction.

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION ENTERPRISES, INC. [3 News That Matters]

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION SECURES SPOT ON FORTUNE 100 BEST COMPANIES TO WORK FOR

PCL Construction Enterprises, Inc. has been recognized as one of the Fortune 100 Best Companies to Work For in 2026. This acknowledgment comes as a testament to PCL's commitment to fostering a high-trust workplace environment, reflecting the company's dedication to employee satisfaction and engagement. The official announcement was made on 2nd April 2026.This marks the 17th time PCL Construction has earned a place on this prestigious list, achieving a notable rank of 91 this year. The recognition highlights PCL's sustained efforts in creating a supportive and empowering work environment, where employees feel valued and motivated to contribute to the company's success.Deron Brown, president and chief operating officer of PCL's U.S. operations, emphasized that the company's employee-owned structure plays a critical role in shaping its culture. He noted, 'Our people are directly invested in shaping a culture where they feel trusted, supported and excited about the future.' This approach not only enhances employee morale but also drives the overall performance of PCL Construction.PCL's recognition comes on the heels of a successful year, during which the company also ranked No. 11 on Engineering News-Record’s Top 400 Contractors list for 2025. This accolade underscores PCL Construction's leadership in executing complex projects across North America while maintaining its focus on employee well-being and satisfaction.The Fortune 100 Best Companies to Work For list serves as a benchmark for organizational health and employee experience. Companies like PCL Construction, which consistently rank high on this list, often see a positive correlation between employee trust and business performance, reinforcing the belief that a supportive workplace is essential for long-term success.

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION TACKLES COLORADO RIVER CRISIS WITH INNOVATIVE WATER SOLUTIONS

As the Colorado River faces unprecedented challenges, PCL Construction Enterprises, Inc. is stepping up to provide innovative solutions aimed at water resilience. On the 26th of March 2026, the urgency surrounding the Colorado River's dwindling water supply became a focal point for communities across the Western United States, as they seek alternatives to secure their water futures.The Colorado River serves as a vital resource for millions, yet it is currently grappling with severe drought conditions. Lake Powell, for instance, is at an alarming 162 feet below full capacity, holding only 26% of its total water volume. The U.S. Bureau of Reclamation warns that if current conditions persist, Lake Mead could reach record lows within two years, prompting a critical reassessment of water management strategies across the basin states.PCL Construction is at the forefront of this reassessment, advocating for investments in water reuse infrastructure as a key strategy for bolstering water security. With projected U.S. investments in water reuse expected to average $47.1 billion over the next decade, PCL is positioned to support municipalities in capturing and reusing water to mitigate the effects of ongoing drought. Although adoption rates vary significantly among the seven Colorado River basin states, PCL's initiatives exemplify how infrastructure investments can enhance resilience in water supply systems.The urgency of the situation is underscored by the imminent expiration of current water management rules in the fall of 2026. As states grapple with the looming deadline to ratify Colorado River water allocation agreements, PCL Construction's projects, such as the Drought Pipeline Program in Phoenix, provide tangible examples of how communities can adapt to a drier future. This program has been crucial in ensuring the reliability of water supply for the rapidly growing Phoenix area, which has seen a 12% population increase over the last decade.Industry leaders, including PCL, recently gathered at the Colorado River Water Users Association Conference to discuss collaborative strategies for securing the river's future. The consensus was clear: a multifaceted approach is necessary to address the complexities of climate change and aging infrastructure. PCL Construction's commitment to advancing water reuse initiatives positions the company as a critical player in shaping sustainable water management practices that can withstand the challenges posed by the Colorado River's ongoing crisis.

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION ADVANCES $192M AI-READY DATA CENTER PROJECT

PCL Construction Services, Inc. and PCL Industrial Services, Inc., operating companies of PCL Construction, have reached structural completion on a $192 million, 22.5-megawatt data center, advancing one of the largest digital infrastructure projects currently underway in the Denver metropolitan area. On 19th March 2026, the companies celebrated this significant milestone that underscores their commitment to delivering state-of-the-art facilities designed for high-capacity digital workloads.Located on a 17-acre site southeast of Denver, the project is being delivered by PCL’s Minneapolis Buildings group in collaboration with the Denver team, combining longstanding client relationships with local market expertise. The data center is specifically designed to support enterprise-scale, compute-intensive workloads, including artificial intelligence and graphics processing unit applications, as demand for high-capacity digital infrastructure continues to grow across the region.PCL’s scope of work includes the phased construction of five steel-frame colocation data halls totaling approximately 277,000 square feet, allowing for efficient onboarding of new tenants while maintaining flexibility for future expansion. The project also features a dedicated administration building with office space for personnel and on-site operations teams. This design aims to accommodate high-density deployments, aligning performance needs with modern sustainability and energy-efficiency requirements.The project includes comprehensive electrical infrastructure to support continuous, high-performance operations, from permanent and temporary power systems to sitewide distribution, security, and communications pathways. Key work includes installing and integrating major power and cooling equipment that enables reliable, high-density data center operations. PCL Industrial Services, Inc. is self-performing the core electrical work while coordinating specialized partners for testing and life-safety systems.PCL Construction’s extensive experience in delivering mission-critical and data center facilities across the United States is critical on complex, high-density projects like this, according to Ryan Schmidt, vice president and district manager of Denver Buildings. The project teams recently marked the topping-out ceremony, celebrating the dedication, craftsmanship, and collaboration that will transition the development into interior build-out and systems installation, with construction scheduled for completion in summer 2027.
