value.Title: DESRI And Meta Announce 850 MW Of Ppas, With Partnership Totaling 2.5+ GW Across Nine States

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJP-4TK3-S32P-N3GW-00000-00DESRI And Meta Announce 850 MW Of Ppas, With Partnership Totaling 2.5+ GW Across Nine States2026-05-12T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:01:08ZLexisNexisDESRI And Meta Announce 850 MW Of Ppas, With Partnership Totaling 2.5+ GW Across Nine States

NEW YORK, May 12, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- DESRI, a leading renewable energy producer in the U.S., and Meta today announced the signing of 850 megawatts (MW) of new power purchase agreements (PPAs) in 2026. These include: 500 MW in Oklahoma; 200 MW in Texas and 150 MW in Mississippi. To date, DESRI and Meta have contracted approximately 2,575 MW of solar and battery storage projects across nine states to support Meta's efforts to add new generation to the grid.


"Our energy infrastructure partnership with Meta now spans nine states and more than two and a half gigawatts across the country," said Hy Martin, Chief Development Officer of DESRI. "These projects support Meta's energy commitments and they will generate significant economic development for local rural economies across the country. Our relationship with large companies like Meta is a cornerstone of DESRI's long-term strategy of delivering cost-effective, reliable energy projects to power the U.S. economy's growth."
"DESRI has been a valued partner as we work to bring new energy to grids across the United States. This expanded partnership - now spanning nine states - reflects our commitment to supporting the communities where we operate," said Amanda Yang, Head of Clean and Renewable Energy at Meta.
In addition to the 2026 signings, other contracted projects include: 150 MW in Texas; 300 MW in Utah; and 100 MW in New Mexico, among others. DESRI expects approximately 1,110 MW of these contracted projects to begin construction this year. Each project is expected to create hundreds of construction jobs and will contribute to the local economy throughout its respective operational term. DESRI regularly includes funding allocations in their PPAs for high school scholarships awarded to students interested in clean energy careers, and are pleased to include similar initiatives in several states.
"Our DESRI team is proud to support the next generation of energy leaders through initiatives like the Virginia Clean Energy Community Leaders Scholarship program that will assist students in pursuing post-secondary education with a focus on energy and STEM," added Hy Martin.
About DESRI
DESRI and its affiliates develop, acquire, invest-in, own, and operate long-term contracted renewable energy assets in the U.S. DESRI's portfolio of contracted, operating, and in-construction renewable energy projects currently includes over 80 solar, wind and storage projects representing more than twelve gigawatts of aggregate capacity. value.Title: April 28, 2026: Swinerton to Lead Construction of San Francisco Fire Department Training Campus in $145M Deal

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHR-F9P3-SCRX-631R-00000-00April 28, 2026: Swinerton to Lead Construction of San Francisco Fire Department Training Campus in $145M Deal2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:01:08ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSApril 28, 2026: Swinerton to Lead Construction of San Francisco Fire Department Training Campus in $145M Deal

Swinerton Builders has been named Construction Manager/General Contractor for a $145 million San Francisco Fire Department training facility, with the contract awarded by San Francisco Public Works. Groundbreaking for the project occurred on 16th April 2026.

The new SFFD Division of Training campus will be built in the city's southeastern district and will include a 50,000-square-foot scenario district that replicates the characteristics of local neighborhoods.

Beyond the scenario district, the center will provide emergency medical service training, onsite fire apparatus training, and Neighborhood Emergency Response Team training sessions to prepare responders for a range of incidents.

The Administration/Classroom Building will be designated as an Essential Services Building to ensure immediate occupancy after a major earthquake and will function as a backup emergency operations center.

Christina Pantera, project executive at Swinerton, noted the firm's extensive San Francisco portfolio, while Paul Hinz, vice president and division manager, emphasized the center's role in strengthening community resilience and safety through close collaboration with city stakeholders. Swinerton's 135-year history underpins its capacity to deliver such a facility.

SECTION 1 SWINERTON INCORPORATED PROFILE

1.1 ACTIVITIES

Swinerton Incorporated provides commercial construction and construction management services in the Western United States. It offers general construction, modernization and renovation, management and consulting, preconstruction, corporate, and renewable energy services. The companys projects include aviation, cultural, education, federal, gaming and entertainment, healthcare, hospitality, interiors, mission critical, mixed-use/residential, office, renewable energy, retail, and special. Swinerton Incorporated was formerly known as Swinerton & Walberg Co. The company was founded in 1888 and is based in San Francisco, California with additional offices in California, Colorado, Hawaii, Texas, New Mexico, and Washington.

urn:contentItem:6JHJ-FR23-RTWG-C008-00000-00April 28, 2026: San Francisco Breaks Ground on Innovative Fire Training Center as Swinerton Takes CM/GC Role2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:01:08ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSApril 28, 2026: San Francisco Breaks Ground on Innovative Fire Training Center as Swinerton Takes CM/GC Role

Swinerton Builders has been named Construction Manager/General Contractor for a $145 million San Francisco Fire Department training facility, with San Francisco Public Works as the client. The groundbreaking took place on 16th April 2026 in the city's southeastern district.

The project centers on a 50,000-square-foot scenario district designed to mirror San Francisco neighborhoods, featuring settings such as sloped intersections and tight corridors that simulate post-earthquake conditions.

The facility will also house emergency medical service training, onsite fire apparatus training, and Neighborhood Emergency Response Team sessions, complementing the scenario district.

An Administration/Classroom Building will be designated as an Essential Services Building to allow immediate occupancy after a major earthquake and to serve as a backup emergency operations center; Swinerton highlights its long history of public safety projects in San Francisco.

urn:contentItem:6JHH-G7C3-SFSX-M180-00000-00SWINERTON INCORPORATED - THE RECENT ON INDUSTRY AND PEER DEVELOPMENTS2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:01:08ZLexisNexisSTOCKSWINERTON INCORPORATED - THE RECENT ON INDUSTRY AND PEER DEVELOPMENTS

DASHBOARD: SWINERTON INCORPORATED

PermID4296594671Websitehttp://www.swinerton.comIndustryConstruction & EngineeringAddress5707 SOUTHWEST PARKWAY, BLDG. 1, SUITE 200 | AUSTIN, TX 78735

ACTIVITIES:

Swinerton Incorporated provides commercial construction and construction management services in the Western United States. It offers general construction, modernization and renovation, management and consulting, preconstruction, corporate, and renewable energy services. The companys projects include aviation, cultural, education, federal, gaming and entertainment, healthcare, hospitality, interiors, mission critical, mixed-use/residential, office, renewable energy, retail, and special. Swinerton Incorporated was formerly known as Swinerton & Walberg Co. The company was founded in 1888 and is based in San Francisco, California with additional offices in California, Colorado, Hawaii, Texas, New Mexico, and Washington.

+ SWINERTON INCORPORATED [3 News That Matters]

+ SWINERTON APPOINTS VETERAN LEADER KRISTEN BEJARANO TO LEAD MISSION CRITICAL GROWTH

Apr. 8, 2026 — Swinerton Incorporated named Kristen Bejarano as Director of Mission Critical, reflecting the company's commitment to expanding its footprint in mission critical and data center infrastructure. Bejarano brings nearly a quarter century of experience delivering complex mission critical work for leading technology companies. She has launched and scaled a national mission critical and data center division and directed strategy and procurement for a major Americas data center program. In the newly created role, Bejarano will drive Swinerton's mission critical strategy, collaborating with clients to translate intricate requirements into practical plans that emphasize speed to market, sustainability, and energy efficiency. She will also build on her track record of developing megaprojects for technology clients. Chany, Swinerton's Chief Revenue Officer, said Bejarano is well positioned to accelerate growth in the mission critical sector while enhancing the client experience. Her focus on cooling technologies and renewable energy integration is expected to yield innovative solutions that align with Swinerton's emphasis on sustainability and operational efficiency. Swinerton, founded in 1888 and wholly employee-owned, includes Swinerton Builders, Timberlab, SAK Builders, and Lindgren Development, serving projects nationwide.

+ SWINERTON NAMES NEW LEADERS TO ACCELERATE NORTHEAST GROWTH

Swinerton Builders announced leadership changes in its Northeast Division, naming Jay Quackenbush to vice president and division manager, effective 16th March 2026. Nicholas Schifferle was appointed director of client relations and Robert Murphy was named project executive, as part of a broader plan to strengthen the firm's Northeast footprint.Quackenbush, a longtime New Yorker with nearly 30 years overseeing complex construction, brings a deep understanding of New York City's building conditions and strong client relationships that position him to lead initiatives from facility management to large-scale ground-up work.Ray Haj, Swinerton's chief operational officer and East Coast regional manager, said Quackenbush’s leadership will expand the division’s capacity to meet client needs, from day-to-day facility services to large-scale projects.Schifferle, who joined Swinerton in 2025, brings more than 20 years of international business development experience, including managing high-profile design and construction projects across Asia and Australia, and founding a San Francisco design-build firm. His ongoing involvement with CoreNet Global underscores a commitment to the commercial real estate sector.Murphy, with his track record overseeing more than $650 million in commercial interiors work, will lead multiple project teams to ensure quality and client satisfaction. He will work alongside division leaders Sloane Brown and Peter Fontana as the Northeast continues to grow its interiors footprint.Since establishing a Northeast presence in 2021, Swinerton’s division has delivered a range of projects across office fit-outs, historic renovations, and ground-up construction, with continued momentum into education, residential and public sectors.

+ DON ADAIR TO RETIRE; SWINERTON NAMES CHRIS CHANY TO LEAD REVENUE GROWTH

Swinerton announced that Executive Vice President and Chief Revenue Officer Don Adair will retire on January 9, 2026, marking the end of a nearly four-decade career in the construction industry. The company cited ongoing growth and strategic development under new leadership, and the retirement was communicated to Swinerton’s employee-owners on June 13, 2025.Adair joined Swinerton Builders in 1988 as a Project Engineer in Los Angeles and later led the San Diego office expansion before being named EVP and CRO in 2019. During his tenure, he helped shape Swinerton’s revenue-generation and client-experience strategies, contributing to overall growth.In his retirement remarks, Adair emphasized pride in Swinerton’s culture and the importance of building strong teams and lasting relationships that underpin long-term success for the company and its partners.Chris Chany, who has worked closely with Adair for nearly a decade, will succeed him. Chany joined Swinerton Builders in 2013 and is recognized for fostering partnerships and driving strategic growth. He said the company will continue to uphold core values while pursuing new growth opportunities.Chany added that the company would focus on strengthening relationships and delivering superior client experiences and reliable project execution, signaling Swinerton’s intent to sustain momentum in the construction sector.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BALFOUR BEATTY [3 News That Matters]

+ TRENT JOHNSON NAMED VICE PRESIDENT OF AVIATION OPERATIONS FOR BALFOUR BEATTY IN THE CAROLINAS

Balfour Beatty promoted Trent Johnson to Vice President of operations for the Carolinas, effective Apr. 1, 2026. In the newly expanded role, he will lead and grow the company's aviation practice across North and South Carolina, leveraging nearly two decades in the construction industry. Johnson brings 12-and-a-half years with Balfour Beatty and a total of 19 years in the field. He has delivered 22 aviation projects, including targeted renovations and multi-phase programmes, with notable work at Raleigh-Durham International Airport and Albert J. Ellis Airport, helping to solidify the company's aviation footprint in the region. In the new role, he will oversee the aviation segment across the Carolinas, continuing to support existing clients while pursuing opportunities at additional regional and international airports. His focus will include guiding project teams through active airport environments, prioritising safety, operational continuity, and stakeholder coordination. Jeff Sandeen, President of Balfour Beatty in the Carolinas, lauded Johnson for building a strong reputation as a trusted leader and client partner, and said his leadership will drive the next phase of growth for the company's aviation portfolio. Johnson holds a degree in Construction Engineering Management from North Carolina State University and has been active in the community, notably with the Make-A-Wish Foundation.

+ MAINMARK UK SECURES ROLE ON BALFOUR BEATTY CP7 FRAMEWORK TO SUPPORT NETWORK RAIL

Mainmark UK has been awarded a place on the Balfour Beatty plc CP7 Framework, positioning the geotechnical contractor to support Network Rail’s CP7 investment programme running from 1st April 2024 to 31st March 2029, with an overall envelope of around £43-45 billion across Great Britain.CP7 is designed to maintain, renew, and modernise the railway, delivering a safer, more reliable, and more sustainable network. Investment priorities include infrastructure maintenance and renewals, digital signalling and technology, safety improvements, and initiatives to improve both freight and passenger performance.Within the CP7 Framework, contractors will undertake a mix of activities such as low complexity emergency callouts, planned preventative maintenance, and small scale civil engineering schemes. Mainmark UK will take on geotechnical response works, bridge repairs, structural integrity projects, platform refurbishments, and drainage clearance, among other tasks.Tom Kavanagh, General Manager at Mainmark UK, commented, "We’re looking forward to collaborating with Balfour Beatty to help deliver critical infrastructure works across the network during the CP7 period. Being selected to support Balfour Beatty on this framework is a fantastic opportunity for our team and aligns perfectly with our growth plans."Mainmark is renowned for its Teretek resin injection technology, which stabilises weak ground and fills subsurface voids with minimal disruption. As heavier freight loads and severe weather stress railway assets, Teretek helps preserve structural integrity and asset resilience. The CP7 appointment positions Mainmark to contribute to a more resilient UK rail network.

+ BALFOUR BEATTY POSTS 9% EPS RISE AS UK-US PROJECTS SUPPORT REVENUE GROWTH

Balfour Beatty PLC posted strong growth, powered by its construction arm, notably power-transmission work. The group’s market value has climbed about 66% over the past year as it rides a record order book, while underlying earnings per share rose 9% to 47.6 pence. On 11 March 2026, the group said top-line growth was supported by UK demand for power transmission and a surge in US building activity. Operating profits in the support services segment jumped a third to £122m, and the underlying margin rose 80 basis points to 8.5%. The UK construction business also exceeded its margin targets ahead of schedule. In the US, rising building volumes helped the group grow, though it faced cost overruns on a Civils project. The company maintains a dual-market strategy designed to sustain profitability amid these challenges. Analysts remain upbeat about the outlook, noting the large and expanding order book amid ongoing infrastructure investment. Balfour Beatty aims to improve margins and deliver projects on time, reinforcing its position in both the UK and US markets. Looking ahead, the company sees opportunity in expanding power grids and broader construction demand, while remaining mindful of rising costs and project-management challenges that could affect near-term performance.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ HENSEL PHELPS CONSTRUCTION CO [3 News That Matters]

+ HENSEL PHELPS SIGNS MULTI-YEAR DEAL WITH TRACK3D TO STANDARDIZE CONSTRUCTION PROGRESS MONITORING ACROSS PORTFOLIO

Track3D and Hensel Phelps have signed a multi-year agreement to roll out Track3D's Reality Intelligence platform across more than 200 active projects, elevating a pilot into a portfolio-wide deployment as of Apr. 1, 2026. The deal underscores Hensel Phelps' commitment to leveraging AI-driven construction data across its operations.The arrangement envisions standardizing Track3D for portfolio-wide progress monitoring, enabling earlier identification of on-site issues and reducing delays on complex projects, while improving schedule reliability and accountability across diverse sites.A San Francisco International Airport case study showcased the platform's ability to move teams from reactive problem-solving to proactive decision-making through 360-degree site walks and active involvement by existing field personnel, minimizing workflow disruption.In its March update, Track3D introduced an admin dashboard, Groups functionality and capture-free progress tracking directly on floorplans, designed to give major contractors centralized visibility and easier user management.The company is expanding its market approach by targeting specialty contractors, with plans to participate in the AWCI Convention & Expo 2026 to demonstrate automation of production tracking and reduction of manual reporting for drywall and ceiling professionals. Taken together, the developments reinforce Track3D's competitive position and lay groundwork for future growth.

+ MICAH (PONO) CHOY NAMED PROJECT SUPERINTENDENT FOR HAWAII REGIONAL KITCHEN AT HENSEL PHELPS

Micah (Pono) Choy has been promoted to project superintendent at Hensel Phelps, overseeing the Hawaii Department of Education Regional Kitchen – North Central Oahu project in Wahiawa. As of 1st April 2026, the promotion marks a milestone in his career with the firm, which he joined in 2018.Choy's experience spans aviation, wastewater, and federal market projects in Hawaii and Guam, where he has helped deliver complex assignments from planning to execution.Choy's collaborative leadership has allowed him to build strong partnerships across teams and consistently drive project outcomes. Hensel Phelps regards this promotion as part of its ongoing effort to develop talent from within; the Regional Kitchen project is essential for upgrading Hawaii's educational facilities and will benefit from his leadership.

+ HENSEL PHELPS ADOPTS TRACK3D REALITY INTELLIGENCE FOR PORTFOLIO-WIDE PROGRESS MONITORING

As of 25th March 2026, Track3D, an AI-driven construction technology company, announced a multi-year enterprise agreement with Hensel Phelps that expands from a pilot to deployment on more than 200 active projects.Hensel Phelps plans to standardize Track3D’s Reality Intelligence platform across its portfolio for progress monitoring, signaling confidence in the technology’s scalability across diverse project types. The extensive rollout is expected to deepen recurring revenue, strengthen customer loyalty, and create a high-profile reference account that could attract additional large contractor deals.In a notable application, Track3D pointed to a San Francisco International Airport case study where Hensel Phelps used the platform to shift from reactive to proactive decision-making, improving progress tracking and coordination across trades without major workflow changes.On the development front, Track3D disclosed a March release to boost enterprise oversight and workflow efficiency, including an admin dashboard and Groups functionality to centralize portfolio visibility and streamline user management, plus capture-free progress tracking on floorplans, enhancing operational flexibility for enterprise users.The company is also sharpening its go-to-market by targeting specialty contractors and will participate in the AWCI Convention & Expo 2026, signaling momentum in the industry as it aims to automate production tracking and protect margins. Together with the Hensel Phelps deal and the SFO case study, the March upgrades mark meaningful commercial traction for Track3D.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ QUANTA SERVICES [3 News That Matters]

+ QUANTA SERVICES COMPLETES TRI-CITY GROUP PURCHASE, BROADENING ELECTRIC INFRASTRUCTURE CAPABILITIES

Quanta Services, Inc. completed the Tri-City Group deal, expanding its footprint in electrical construction and industrial services. The Davenport-based contractor's acquisition, announced on 6th April 2026, strengthens Quanta's capacity to serve electric power and renewable energy programs across the United States.The Tri-City Group has a long history in the Quad Cities dating to 1895 and rebranded in 2024 to reflect its diversified services. It includes Tri-City Electric and Tri-City Renewable Energy, with a presence across 46 states and several countries.Lori Rochholz of Tri-City reaffirmed that the brand will remain unchanged, and leadership from the Palmer Family will stay in place, ensuring continuity and client service. The company remains committed to the local community.Quanta's CEO Duke Austin noted the acquisition, along with Wilson Construction Co. and Billings Flying Service, broadens its crafts-skilled platform to support electric utility programs nationwide. The deal expands Quanta’s 69,000-employee platform to meet growing demand in infrastructure markets.The deal is expected to bring operational synergies as Quanta integrates Tri-City's subsidiaries and regional capabilities, strengthening its leadership in electrical infrastructure.

+ QUANTA SEES GROWTH ACROSS CLEAN-ENERGY BUILDOUT, DETAILS PLAN AT JEFFERIES

Quanta Services used the Jefferies conference to lay out its strategic vision for 2026, presenting on Mar. 3, 2026 after a solid year. Executives highlighted favorable tailwinds from clean-energy deployment and infrastructure development as growth drivers and outlined steps to strengthen operations and broaden service offerings. The discussion touched on the data-center market, with the company pointing to partnerships that reflect a measured approach to risk management. By engaging with mid-size utilities such as NIPSCO, Quanta aims to tailor solutions that meet specific needs while mitigating counterparty risk. Management reiterated a disciplined focus on organic growth and capital allocation, noting that acquisitions will be selective and aimed at sustainable, long-term value. Overall, the company portrayed a proactive stance to navigate a rapidly changing industry and position for growth in 2026.

+ QUANTA SERVICES EMERGES AS THE CENTRAL PLATFORM FOR UTILITY MODERNIZATION AND GRID RESILIENCE

Quanta Services has established itself as the central infrastructure platform for utilities, renewables and grid modernization, with the company poised to be a key execution engine in North America's energy transition by year-end 2025. Its reach extends beyond consumer brands to power lines, substations, pipelines and fiber networks that underpin an increasingly electrified economy.The firm emphasizes engineering, procurement and construction (EPC) services, enabling utilities to outsource entire projects and reduce the complexity of coordinating multiple contractors. This EPC-led model is designed to accelerate modernization while simplifying project execution and risk allocation.Quanta operates through three core lines—electric power infrastructure, underground utility and energy infrastructure, and communications infrastructure—providing an integrated platform capable of handling high-voltage transmission, urban undergrounding and modern communications networks. The company leverages technologies such as LiDAR and grid digitalization to boost efficiency and strengthen risk management for its utility customers.In a competitive field that includes MasTec and MYR Group, Quanta differentiates itself through scale and focused specialization in utility and network infrastructure, enabling it to win large-scale modernization programs more consistently than peers.From an investor perspective, Quanta Services' stock (ISIN US74762E1029) carries a premium valuation relative to traditional engineering peers, reflecting confidence in its ability to deliver durable growth as utilities accelerate grid hardening and renewable integration.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BARTON MALOW COMPANY [3 News That Matters]

+ BARTON MALOW, MARCH-WESTIN TO MANAGE MILAN PUSKAR STADIUM WEST TOWER

MORGANTOWN, W.Va. - West Virginia University's Department of Intercollegiate Athletics will partner with Barton Malow and March-Westin to serve as construction managers on the Milan Puskar Stadium West Tower Project, which is set to begin after the 2026 football season. The joint venture brings together a power team of national and local construction leadership to the project.As construction managers, Barton Malow and March-Westin will oversee all construction from conception to completion, ensuring that the project is finished safely, on time and within budget. By managing the daily on-site operations, the team will be the primary liaison between the university, architects and engineers, handling preconstruction planning, cost and schedule management, procurement, and coordination of onsite construction activities.The project team will draw on the experience of both firms to deliver a functional and iconic addition to Milan Puskar Stadium, with design and operations considerations aligned to the university's needs.The collaboration combines complementary expertise in the unique campus and stadium environments. March-Westin brings decades of experience delivering work at Milan Puskar Stadium and across the university's two main campuses, while Barton Malow contributes extensive experience leading collegiate and professional stadium renovations nationwide.Jamie Ridgeway, president of March-Westin, noted that the local knowledge paired with Barton Malow's national experience uniquely positions the team to successfully complete this project with minimal disruption to the fan experience.

+ DETROIT CITY FC NAMES BARTON MALOW CONSTRUCTION MANAGER FOR ALUMNIFI FIELD

Barton Malow has been selected as construction manager for AlumniFi Field, Detroit City FC's forthcoming 15,000-seat home stadium to be built on the former Southwest Detroit Hospital site. The project aims for a spring 2027 opening.Detroit City FC is expanding its presence in the USL Championship, with fan demand at the current 7,000-seat Keyworth Stadium regularly exceeding capacity.AlumniFi Field will sit between Detroit's Corktown and Mexicantown neighborhoods and is designed to function as a vibrant community hub capable of hosting soccer matches as well as concerts and other events.Design plans include a natural grass pitch, three permanent grandstands with canopies, and dedicated spaces for community activities and other uses of the multi-purpose venue.The hospital's legacy in promoting diversity in hiring since it opened in 1973 will be honored as part of the project, and officials say the stadium will reflect Detroit's character and strengthen ties with local supporters.

+ MACON ARENA SUBCONTRACTOR BRIEFING SET BY BARTON MALOW, SHERIDAN

Barton Malow and Sheridan Construction will host an information session for subcontractors and suppliers on the New Macon Arena project, with the aim of presenting the project scope, schedule, and available trade bid opportunities for firms including SLBE, MBE, WBE, and DBE participants. The session is set for 24th March 2026, from 11:00 a.m. to 12:30 p.m. at the Macon Centreplex, 200 Coliseum Drive, Macon.Attendees will hear a short presentation, followed by a Q&A and networking time with project leads. Registration is encouraged but not required; firms can register ahead of time through the Barton Malow website.The Office of Small Business Affairs (OSBA) in Macon-Bibb County supports the outreach and invites local vendors to participate. OSBA Director Charise Stephens-Merriweather notes that the project offers a unique opportunity for local, small, and minority businesses to bid from the outset.Mayor Lester Miller described the arena as more than a venue, calling it a destination and major economic engine that will attract visitors and help keep the city competitive for years to come.The new Macon Arena will be built on the current Macon Coliseum site, spanning more than 230,000 square feet with seating for about 8,500 for concerts. The design emphasizes flexibility to accommodate sports and other events, and Barton Malow and Sheridan Construction bring a long track record of delivering high-quality projects and community-focused development.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION TO GROW INNSBROOK FOOTPRINT WITH 9,100-SF EXPANSION BACKED BY HENRICO COUNTY

DPR Construction announced a 9,100-square-foot expansion of its Innsbrook office, supported by Henrico County incentives to attract and retain businesses. The date of this announcement is 29th April 2026.The expansion aims to accommodate a growing workforce and strengthen operational capabilities as part of the company’s regional growth strategy.The project will bring together teams and resources, promoting collaboration and innovation across the organization.Sustainable design features and modern office concepts will be integrated, aligning with DPR’s leadership in sustainable construction.Completion is expected within the next year, underscoring the company’s commitment to the region and its ongoing community initiatives.

+ JASON PAGE APPOINTED HEAD OF DPR CONSTRUCTION'S NASHVILLE SPECIAL SERVICES GROUP

The Special Services Group handles a broad range of work, from targeted improvements like parking garage stair installations to expansive interior buildouts for new office and entertainment spaces, and Page's leadership is expected to expand the group's capabilities to meet evolving client and stakeholder needs in Nashville. Beyond project leadership, Page mentors and coaches teams, sharing best practices for quick-turn, high-impact work and fostering a culture of collaboration and innovation as DPR expands its Nashville footprint.

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION SECURES GREAT PLACE TO WORK STATUS AS STAFF REPORT HIGH ENGAGEMENT

DPR Construction has secured Great Place To Work Certification, confirming that 97 percent of its employees say it is a great place to work, a figure about 40 points higher than the national average. The award, presented on 31st March 2026, signals a culture rooted in pride, belonging and opportunity across the firm’s projects.CEO George Pfeffer noted the certification reflects sentiments across the organization. "Being Certified(TradeMark) as a great place to work is especially meaningful because it comes directly from our people, who bring our culture to life," he said. The company emphasizes trust, support, and growth to help employees perform at their best.DPR’s operating model empowers staff to make decisions and share information openly, encouraging initiative and cross functional collaboration on complex tasks, new roles, and ideas beyond job descriptions. A strong emphasis on learning and development supports clear career paths aligned with individual aspirations.Great Place To Work® research aligns with these findings, highlighting a sense of pride and belonging among workers. The certification has helped DPR strengthen its reputation as an employer of choice, while external accolades from U.S. News & World Report and Newsweek have recognized DPR in more than 15 U.S. cities over the last year.Founded in 1990, DPR has evolved into a multi billion dollar contractor known for complex and sustainable projects across sectors. Its innovation posture—through virtual design and construction and self performing craft—helps explain its roughly 11,000 person workforce worldwide. More information about DPR’s culture and career opportunities is available on its official website.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ TURNER CELEBRATES TOPPING OUT OF 72-MW ALIGNED IAD-06 DATA CENTER IN FREDERICK

Turner Construction Company marked the topping out of the Aligned IAD-06 Data Center in Frederick, Maryland, signifying the completion of the structure for the 72-megawatt facility. The milestone was achieved on the 28th of April 2026, less than five months after ground had been broken. To date, the project has logged more than 80,000 craft hours, with roughly 345 skilled workers contributing to progress. The 450,000-square-foot center sits on a 75-acre campus and features a two-story structural steel design, an insulated precast exterior system, and a high-efficiency hybrid cooling solution that combines liquid and air-cooled technologies to optimize performance. Turner's leadership emphasized that topping out represents more than erecting the frame, underscoring the strength of the partnership with Aligned and the team’s commitment to safe, efficient delivery. As the project moves into interior fit-out and operational readiness, stakeholders will watch closely, anticipating the facility’s role in supporting the region’s digital infrastructure.

+ GEISINGER WYOMING VALLEY EXPANSION MOVES TO VERTICAL CONSTRUCTION AS TURNER BREAKS GROUND

WILKES-BARRE, PA — Turner Construction has advanced to vertical construction on Geisinger Wyoming Valley Medical Center's $900 million expansion, moving forward after years of planning and foundation work. Steel now rises on an 11-story, 600,000-square-foot patient tower that will lift beds from 356 to 500 and expand operating rooms from 12 to 18. Officials say the addition will enlarge the emergency department and ensure all patient rooms are private, with completion expected in the first quarter of 2028. The date of this announcement is 28th April 2026.David Kaminski, Turner’s Vice President and Senior General Manager, described the milestone as a key step toward broader health care access for families in northeastern Pennsylvania and a chance to partner with Geisinger on a project that strengthens the community for decades. The project is projected to create as many as 3,800 construction jobs and generate about $1 billion in local economic impact. Onsite employment is expected to rise from 150 workers to as many as 450 during peak activity, with Geisinger planning to hire an additional 400 staff after opening.Geisinger Wyoming Valley's campus opened in 1981 and has since expanded to meet rising demand for medical services. The new tower will bring advanced capabilities comparable to those typically found in major academic medical centers serving large metropolitan areas.

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION NAMED BUILDER FOR WESTCOURT ORLANDO'S AMBITIOUS MIXED-USE DISTRICT

Turner Construction has been selected to build Westcourt Orlando, the long-planned mixed-use district just north of the Kia Center, with enabling work already underway as of 22nd April 2026.The decision followed an extensive preconstruction phase. Todd Chapman, president of JMA Ventures, credited Turner's track record on complex builds, particularly in the Space Coast, as a key factor in securing the contract. "If you guys can send things into space, I’m pretty sure you can work with us and build this project," Chapman remarked, underscoring confidence in Turner's capabilities.Turner has been involved in pre-construction activities since 2022, recently launching site operations including utility mapping and surveying.With a strong local footprint dating to 1984, Turner’s Orlando office and regional leadership under vice president Jeff Justen were cited by developers as important factors in the decision.Westcourt is envisioned as a sprawling 900,000 to 1,000,000-square-foot district that will integrate hotel, residential, office, retail, and live event spaces adjacent to the Kia Center. The plans include a 265-key Kimpton hotel, approximately 269 high-rise residences, around 300,000 square feet of Class A office space, 125,000 square feet of retail, a 3,500-capacity events venue, and roughly 1,140 parking stalls.While a firm groundbreaking date has yet to be confirmed, published timelines indicate a projected start in Q1 2026, potentially completing by late 2028, although local sources suggest delays could push mobilization to the end of 2026. The development team anticipates that Turner Construction will support approximately 800 workers on-site at peak construction, contributing around 1,600 direct jobs throughout the project’s lifecycle. As office leasing for the Class A spaces has already commenced, developers report that a significant portion is pre-leased, which could facilitate financing and scheduling as they continue to update the public on the progress of the site.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION ENTERPRISES, INC. [2 News That Matters]

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION SECURES 17TH PLACEMENT ON FORTUNE'S 100 BEST EMPLOYERS

PCL Construction Enterprises, Inc. has been named to Fortune's 100 Best Companies to Work For for 2026, with the official announcement dated 2nd April 2026. The recognition signals PCL's ongoing emphasis on a high-trust workplace, employee engagement and satisfaction.This year marks the 17th time PCL has earned a place on the Fortune list, ranking No. 91. The designation relates to its employee-owned structure, which helps cultivate a culture where staff feel valued and motivated.Industry observers note that Fortune's list serves as a benchmark for organizational health and employee experience, and high rankings often align with stronger business performance.Earlier in 2025, PCL also ranked No. 11 on Engineering News-Record's Top 400 Contractors list, reflecting leadership in delivering complex projects across North America while maintaining a focus on people.The combination of these recognitions underscores PCL's strategy of balancing project excellence with employee well-being and satisfaction.

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION ADVANCES WATER-REUSE INITIATIVES AMID COLORADO RIVER CRISIS

On the 26th of March 2026, the Western United States confronted a mounting Colorado River crisis as drought tightens water supplies.Lake Powell sits 162 feet below full and holds only 26% of its capacity; the U.S. Bureau of Reclamation warns Mead could reach record lows within two years, forcing a reevaluation of basin-wide management.PCL Construction is advocating for larger investments in water reuse infrastructure to bolster resilience. They note that projected U.S. investments in water reuse are averaging about $47.1 billion annually over the next decade, underscoring the potential for municipalities to capture and reuse water.Adoption of reuse varies across the seven basin states, underscoring the need for scalable infrastructure and coordinated policy.As the fall 2026 deadline for water-management rules approaches, PCL points to its Drought Pipeline Program in Phoenix as a tangible model for maintaining supply for a growing city, which has seen a 12% population increase over the last decade. At the Colorado River Water Users Association Conference, industry leaders called for a multifaceted strategy to address climate change and aging infrastructure.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ JE DUNN CONSTRUCTION GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ JE DUNN LAUNCHES FORM OFF-SITE SOLUTIONS TO ACCELERATE CONSTRUCTION MANUFACTURING

JE Dunn Construction has launched Form Off-Site Solutions, its off-site manufacturing initiative focused on Design for Manufacturing (DfM) for complex construction components. The program, announced with a launch date of 6th April 2026, builds on the company’s century-long Kansas City heritage while extending capabilities nationwide.Form Off-Site Solutions structures its offerings into four product segments: metal assemblies, wood assemblies, building skin assemblies, and multi-trade assemblies, designed to meet the varied needs of owners, general contractors, trade partners, and original equipment manufacturers.The end-to-end service model covers estimating, design, manufacturing engineering, project management, and production, all aimed at improving installation efficiency, safety, and quality, while providing greater cost certainty and helping shorten project timelines amid labor shortages.Off-Site Manufacturing Director Nick Effenheim emphasizes collaboration: "Anything Form designs, our people can build. Our ability to collaborate with project teams and focus on getting their ideas, concepts, and design into a manufactured environment is something that sets us apart."JE Dunn President and CEO Gordon Lansford says the Form launch marks a major step in the firm’s ongoing push to drive innovation for clients as Form’s production facilities in Kansas City and Nashville position the unit to serve a national client base.

+ STEEL BEAM PLACED AS NORTHLAND CENTER REACHES TOPPING-OUT MILESTONE

KANSAS CITY, Mo. — The topping-out of the Northland Workforce Development Center was celebrated on 19th March 2026, as JE Dunn Construction placed the final structural steel beam for the 145,000-square-foot, two-story facility.Before the beam was hoisted into place, a beam-signing ceremony brought together the Platte County R-3 School District, partner districts, project stakeholders, and community members who signed the beam bearing project and partner logos. Brad Hokanson, Project Executive at JE Dunn Construction, said, 'This project is about creating opportunities and pathways for the next generation of skilled professionals in our region.'The center is being built to house programs in skilled trades, manufacturing, health sciences, and human services, expanding capacity for Northland Career Center and partner institutions while serving both high school students and adult learners seeking credentials. Planned spaces include flexible labs, classrooms, hands-on training environments, and technology-rich areas that reflect real-world workplaces.Located in the Northland region of Kansas City, the project is progressing on schedule, with substantial completion targeted in 2027 and a summer opening expected after the milestone. Executive Director Brian Noller of Platte County School District noted that state, local government, business, investor, and community foundation support have enabled a long-term investment in students and the region.JE Dunn is serving as construction manager, working in partnership with the school district and Clark & Enersen, with a focus on safety, schedule adherence, and coordination while maintaining open communication with stakeholders and minimizing disruption to nearby facilities.

+ JE DUNN REINSTATES CHRIS CHELETTE AS SENIOR VP, HEALTHCARE, TO EXPAND REGIONAL PRESENCE

JE Dunn Construction has announced the return of Chris Chelette, who will serve as the Senior Vice President – Healthcare. Chelette's reappointment comes after he spent nine years as the Senior Vice President of Facilities Services at Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta. This announcement was made on 13th June 2025. During his previous 17-year tenure with JE Dunn, Chelette played a crucial role in expanding the company's healthcare business unit. His extensive experience includes overseeing numerous high-profile projects across the United States, particularly within the non-profit and for-profit healthcare sectors. His return to JE Dunn is seen as a strategic move to enhance the company's healthcare presence in the southeastern region. In his new role, Chelette will be responsible for providing strategic leadership and operational oversight for healthcare construction projects throughout the southeast. Jeff Fuller, President of Healthcare at JE Dunn, expressed enthusiasm about Chelette's return, noting his visionary leadership and impactful experience in the healthcare field. Fuller emphasized that Chelette brings a national perspective and a proven track record that will benefit JE Dunn's future endeavors in healthcare construction. Throughout his career, Chelette has been instrumental in managing significant projects, such as the development of the North Druid Hills campus for Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta. This project, which totaled over $2 billion, featured the Center for Advanced Pediatrics, along with a state-of-the-art 446-bed replacement hospital that opened last year. Such accomplishments highlight Chelette's capability to manage complex healthcare construction initiatives effectively. As JE Dunn continues to invest in its healthcare construction division, Chelette's return is expected to drive growth and innovation in the company's project portfolio. The organization aims to leverage his expertise to enhance its service offerings and solidify its position as a leader in the healthcare construction industry.

+ MCCARTHY HOLDINGS [3 News That Matters]

+ AUDDIA AND THRAMANN CREATE MCCARTHY FINNEY, INC. IN STRATEGIC MERGER

Post-merger ownership is projected to be about 20% for Auddia and 80% for Thramann, subject to a minimum cash balance of $12 million at closing. The special preferred stock issued to Thramann carries a liquidation preference of at least $20.5 million and features flexible conversion and redemption terms, affording Thramann substantial governance influence over the merged entity.

+ CALIFORNIA TOWER TOPPING OUT SIGNALS PROGRESS IN UC DAVIS HEALTH MASTER PLAN

The California Tower is a 14-story hospital with a five-story pavilion designed to house operating rooms, an imaging center, and updated facilities for pharmacy and burn care, and will add about 334 inpatient beds. The tower is part of UC Davis Health's eight-year plan to double the campus size — the largest health-system capital expansion underway in the United States — with opening anticipated in 2030 as the project advances under Vision 2030. The initiative will expand campus space from roughly 3.6 million square feet to more than 7 million.

+ MCCARTHY HOLDINGS DETAILS 2024 EMISSIONS FOOTPRINT IN COMPREHENSIVE GHG REPORT

McCarthy Holdings, one of the largest employee-owned construction companies in the United States, released its updated 2024 GHG Emissions Report, published on 16th February 2026. Developed in partnership with Gravity, the report offers a thorough view of the company’s carbon footprint, covering Scope 1, Scope 2 and key Scope 3 emissions, with Scopes 1 and 2 undergoing independent auditing and limited assurance.The publication aligns with McCarthy’s mission to strengthen sustainability across its operations and subsidiaries and to uphold the standard of being the best builder in America. The company’s sustainability framework rests on three priorities: understanding environmental impact, acting responsibly for people and communities, and efficiently managing energy consumption throughout projects and operations.McCarthy also describes how emissions data informs broader responsible practices, including biodiversity initiatives and responsible fuel management. The organization leverages digital tools and artificial intelligence to improve project decisions and efficiency. A notable initiative is its PV recycling program for solar panels, conducted with SEIA-approved recyclers to divert waste from landfills and advance a circular economy.

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC [3 News That Matters]

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP APPOINTS CARLY REINER CACERES AS NATIONAL CAMPUS RECRUITING HR DIRECTOR

Clark Construction Group appointed Carly Reiner Caceres as Director of Human Resources – National Campus Recruiting, effective Apr. 29, 2026, with a mandate to advance national campus recruiting initiatives and strengthen early-career talent pipelines for its broad portfolio of projects.Caceres arrives from Proper, where she served as Vice President of People Operations, shaping HR strategy and operations in a hybrid workplace. Her background also includes leadership roles at MILLER as Director of Internal Operations and more than nine years at Accenture as Talent Strategy Manager and Campus Recruiting Lead.Her prior experience at Grant Thornton LLP overseeing campus recruiting strategies further reinforces her capability to build robust internship programs and solid student engagement initiatives.Clark Construction Group, established in 1906, has a long track record across education and infrastructure markets. The company says Caceres will bring strategic HR expertise to attract top graduates and mentor the next generation of construction professionals.Her early career featured internships at Charleston Magazine and the Smithsonian Institution’s Hirshhorn Museum, rounding out a diverse background in communications and external relations that will help guide talent development efforts.

+ RENFREWSHIRE COUNCIL AWARDS MULTI-TRADES FRAMEWORK CONTRACT TO CLARK CONTRACTS FOR DOMESTIC HOUSING WORKS

Clark Contracts has been appointed to Renfrewshire Council's multi-trades contractors framework to improve delivery of repair, maintenance and refurbishment services for domestic properties across the authority. The appointment was announced on 11th February 2026 and the framework runs through 2027.The framework covers a broad range of multi-trade works, enabling the council to manage both responsive and planned maintenance more effectively across its housing stock and ensuring residents benefit from a faster, more reliable service.Clark Contracts is a well-established public-sector supplier, with current agreements including Scotland Excel’s New Build Residential Framework and NHS National Services Scotland’s Minor Works Framework. The company also participates in the Crown Commercial Service’s Construction Framework and several Scottish Procurement Alliance frameworks.Gordon Cunningham, managing director at Clark Contracts, said the appointment builds on a track record of more than £200 million in refurbishment projects over the past three years and demonstrates the firm’s commitment to serving local communities.The deal supports Clark Contracts’ growing public-sector footprint as it works with councils and institutions such as the University of Glasgow and Glasgow City Council to deliver essential housing services.

+ CDC APPROVES AMENDMENT FOR ₱4-B THE VILLAGES DEVELOPMENT, PKR JOINS CLARK GROWTH DRIVE

Clark Freeport is set to host a ₱4-billion mixed-use residential and commercial development in The Villages after Clark Development Corp. approved an amendment to PKR Corp.'s lease on December 13, 2025. The revised lease provides PKR with a 30-month window to complete construction and includes safeguards aimed at ensuring on-time delivery. Atty. Agnes VST Devanadera, CDC president and CEO, noted that the amendment reinforces investor confidence and the efficient execution of projects, while keeping The Villages as a community hub where residents can live and participate. PKR Corp. president Daekwon Jung thanked CDC for the partnership and said the company will deliver the Clark project, with PKR operating under Kyeryong Construction Industrial Co., Ltd., a South Korean firm known for safety, technology, and workplace practices. The initiative fits CDC's broader push to accelerate investments, promote well-planned communities, and establish a business-friendly environment that appeals to both global and local investors.

urn:contentItem:6JH3-HP23-S4M4-M32N-00000-00Q&A: Governor Candidates2026-05-03T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:01:08ZLexisNexisAPg. 5Q&A: Governor CandidatesThe New Mexican

Sam Bregman

Age: 62

Party affiliation: Democrat

Educational background: University of New Mexico, Bachelor of Arts in economics, Juris Doctorate

{{tncms-asset app="editorial" id="e4cd91ca-dc6c-4709-9076-ebc436a98ce3"}}

Occupation: District attorney, 2nd Judicial District

Political experience: Albuquerque city councilor, 1995-99; Democratic Party of New Mexico chairman, 2013-15

Relevant life experience: Practicing attorney for over 37 years, specializing in constitutional law; small business owner

Have you ever been charged or convicted of a crime, including drunken driving?: Yes

If yes, please explain: When I was 18, I was charged with a misdemeanor, but the charges were dropped.

Have you ever filed for bankruptcy or been involved in a bankruptcy proceeding, either personally or in business?: No

Have you ever been the subject of liens for unpaid taxes?: No

What sets you apart from the other candidates?

What sets me apart from the other candidates is the toughness and experience the next governor will need to take on the Trump administration. I’m focused on results, not rhetoric, and I have proposed detailed policy plans, not soundbites. I bring a proven track record of delivering for families and working people as a DA and attorney. I have won tough fights against criminals and abusive corporations. I’m ready on day one to tackle critical issues such as housing, cost of living and public safety. I combine pragmatic policy with moral commitment. Our government needs to work for the people.

What should the state government do to stem rising home sales and rental costs?

We have to build more housing fast and make it affordable. That means setting clear production goals, cutting red tape, and putting hard deadlines on permitting so homes get built. We should incentivize starter homes, convert unused commercial space into housing and expand modular construction. My housing plan details this and includes down payment assistance for buyers, a major barrier to ownership. We must build affordable rentals. As renters become homeowners, apartment supply opens, lowering rents. I will crack down on corporate speculation, increase transparency in bulk buying and expand rental assistance. More supply, smarter policy, fairness.

What are your views on data centers, and what should the state do to regulate them?

If done right, data centers can support economic growth and accelerate the development of clean energy. With smart regulation and engaged communities, we can ensure we get the benefits without the long-term costs. Data centers can bring jobs and investment to a community, but they must serve the public interest. We need statewide standards on water use, energy consumption and grid impact. Projects should prioritize renewable energy, pay their fair share for infrastructure and be sited responsibly. Communities must have a voice. I will not support projects that don’t meet environmental standards or raise energy prices.

If elected, would you ensure the continuation of taxpayer-funded childcare for all?

Yes, with a focus on sustainability and access. Childcare is essential infrastructure for working families and our economy. We should build on existing progress by expanding supply, supporting providers with better pay, and ensuring quality care in every community, especially rural and tribal areas. The goal is simple: Every family that wants childcare should be able to find and afford it. That’s how we support parents, strengthen the workforce and give kids a strong start.

Do you plan to continue to follow the roadmap laid out by Gov. Michelle Lujan Grisham in her 50-year water action plan or would you plan major changes to the state's water policy?

We should build on the existing 50-year water roadmap, but with greater urgency, accountability and modernization. Water is our most critical resource, and every policy decision must reflect that. I support stronger conservation, modern infrastructure, and smarter use across agriculture, industry, and communities including expanded water reuse, desalination, and “fit for purpose” use of treated produced water where safe and appropriate. Improve coordination by consolidating water management functions under a more streamlined Department of Natural Resources model. Every decision on housing, energy, and economic development must align with long-term water goals.

Deb Haaland

Age: 65

Party affiliation: Democrat

Educational background: Bachelor's degree in English, University of New Mexico, Juris Doctorate from the UNM School of Law

{{tncms-asset app="editorial" id="1ee5b667-4927-49d7-a6eb-7f1aaded0184"}}

Occupation: Consultant

Political experience: Tribal administrator for San Felipe, chair of the New Mexico Democratic Party, former congresswoman for New Mexico’s 1st Congressional District and Cabinet secretary of the U.S. Department of the Interior

Relevant life experience: Raised my child in New Mexico, was a small business owner, 35th generation New Mexican, attended and graduated from New Mexico public schools

Have you ever been charged or convicted of a crime, including drunken driving?: Yes

If yes, please explain: I have been transparent about my journey with alcohol addiction, and am proud of the 35 years of sobriety I have achieved.

Have you ever filed for bankruptcy or been involved in a bankruptcy proceeding, either personally or in business?: No

Have you ever been the subject of liens for unpaid taxes?: Yes

If yes, please explain: I immediately resolved the lien when I was made aware that it existed.

What sets you apart from the other candidates?

I am the only candidate with legislative and executive experience, serving as a member of Congress and the secretary of the interior, where I oversaw a department of 70,000 people and an $18 billion budget. I also understand the challenges New Mexicans face because I have lived them. I was raised in a rural community, raised my child as a single mom, depended on SNAP and Medicaid, and started a small business. I will use that experience to fight for New Mexicans every day to lower costs, provide a quality education, protect access to healthcare, and make us safer.

What should the state government do to stem rising home sales and rental costs?

New Mexico must aggressively build more housing to lower prices and rental costs. On day one, I will convene a response team to identify and eliminate barriers in permitting, zoning and construction that stall development. We must also protect neighborhoods; I will ban Wall Street firms from buying up our "main streets" by limiting corporate ownership of single-family homes. Additionally, I will implement a "shot clock" rule for housing projects. This mandates timely decisions on approvals or denials, preventing the costly delays that drive up prices. By cutting red tape and prioritizing families over investors, we’ll make New Mexico affordable.

What are your views on data centers, and what should the state do to regulate them?

I will bring opportunities to New Mexico while holding companies accountable for the promises they have made and aren’t emptying the pockets of New Mexicans. We cannot allow new developments to abuse our electrical grid or water supply. I will enact guardrails requiring data centers to utilize sustainable practices, such as closed-loop cooling systems and safe water decontamination, to protect our precious fresh water. Furthermore, I’ll require projects to offset resource usage through renewable energy and water restoration. By prioritizing community benefits, we can grow our tech economy without compromising New Mexico’s long-term economic future for all residents.

If elected, would you ensure the continuation of taxpayer-funded childcare for all?

Yes. I was incredibly proud when New Mexico became the first state to establish universal childcare. I know exactly how difficult it is to afford quality care. When I was raising my children, I worked as a janitor to secure reduced tuition for their childcare. No parent should have to work an extra job just to afford the care that allows them to work. As governor, I will protect and continue these programs. Ensuring every family has access to quality childcare is an economic necessity that supports our workforce, empowers our parents, and gives every child a fair start.

Do you plan to continue to follow the roadmap laid out by Gov. Michelle Lujan Grisham in her 50-year water action plan or would you plan major changes to the state's water policy?

Yes. The 50-year Water Action Plan puts New Mexico in the best position to address our growing water shortage. However, this is only a first step. I will expand these investments by coordinating conservation resources to ensure agencies no longer operate in silos. While we have made great strides, we must ensure future economic development does not deplete our fresh water supply. Additionally, I will prioritize expanding clean water infrastructure in tribal and rural communities. Every New Mexican deserves access to safe drinking water. By modernizing our infrastructure and protecting our precious natural resources, we will build healthier communities today.

Gregg Hull

Age: 59

Party affiliation: Republican

Educational background: High school diploma, Pagosa Springs High School

Occupation: Mayor of Rio Rancho (2014–26); entrepreneur (2000-12)

Political experience: Mayor of Rio Rancho, 2014–26 (three terms; longest-serving mayor in city history, Rio Rancho’s first full-time mayor). Chairman, New Mexico Mayors Caucus (eight years). Board president, New Mexico Municipal League.

{{tncms-asset app="editorial" id="8cb9a919-c636-4035-a015-c9754c33f75d"}}

Relevant life experience: New Mexico resident for over 40 years. Started and operated two successful businesses. Father of five children, all educated in New Mexico public schools. Grandfather of nine. Recipient, Legacy Leadership Award from the Rio Rancho Regional Chamber of Commerce. Graduate of Leadership New Mexico

Have you ever been charged or convicted of a crime, including drunken driving?: No

Have you ever filed for bankruptcy or been involved in a bankruptcy proceeding, either personally or in business?: No

Have you ever been the subject of liens for unpaid taxes?: No

What sets you apart from the other candidates?

I’m the only candidate in this race with executive experience leading a major New Mexico city. Over 12 years as mayor of Rio Rancho, I grew the city from 93,000 to nearly 113,000 residents, secured more than $6 billion in private investment, doubled the police budget, and kept the sales tax rate unchanged since 2014. I’ve worked across party lines as chairman of the New Mexico Mayors Caucus and president of the Municipal League. I’m not a career politician — I’m a businessman and mayor who delivers results. I’ll bring that same proven, pragmatic leadership to the Governor’s Office.

What should the state government do to stem rising home sales and rental costs?

Rising housing costs are fundamentally a supply problem. The state needs to reduce regulatory barriers that delay construction and drive up costs for builders and buyers alike. I support streamlining permitting processes, partnering with cities and counties to identify land suitable for development, and using targeted incentives to encourage workforce housing construction. We must also invest in infrastructure — roads, water, broadband — so communities outside our largest metros can grow sustainably. In Rio Rancho, we proved that a pro-growth, pro-business approach attracts investment and creates housing options for families. I’ll apply that same strategy statewide.

What are your views on data centers, and what should the state do to regulate them?

Data centers represent economic opportunity, but they must be sited responsibly. A project like Project Jupiter would have been far less controversial in southeastern New Mexico, where produced water could cool facilities instead of straining freshwater supplies near population centers. I support data center development as part of economic diversification, but with clear requirements around water use, energy impact and community input. Siting decisions should account for local infrastructure capacity, not just land availability. With the right framework, New Mexico can attract this industry while protecting our water, grid and communities.

If elected, would you ensure the continuation of taxpayer-funded childcare for all?

I’ll evaluate the current program’s outcomes and costs, and ensure we’re directing resources where they’re needed most while preventing potential waste, fraud and abuse we’ve seen in other states in recent months. My priority is making sure low-income working families have real options. We should also reduce regulatory barriers for childcare providers so we can expand capacity and bring costs down. Taxpayer investments in childcare must be accountable, targeted, and built to last.

Do you plan to continue to follow the roadmap laid out by Gov. Michelle Lujan Grisham in her 50-year water action plan or would you plan major changes to the state's water policy?

I share the urgency behind the nonpartisan 50-year water action plan and support several of its key elements, including brackish water development and infrastructure investment. My administration will build on that foundation while adding stronger emphasis on execution and assisting communities in upgrading water systems and developing aquifer recharge systems to optimize resource usage. I’ll also prioritize resolving tribal water rights settlements, upgrading aging systems to reduce waste and ensuring farmers have reliable access. New Mexico cannot afford to plan without delivering. I’ll keep what works, fix what doesn’t, and make sure results follow the rhetoric.

Duke Rodriguez

Age: 68

Party affiliation: Republican

Educational background: Bachelor's degree from New Mexico State University; master's degree in adult education

Occupation: CEO

{{tncms-asset app="editorial" id="965241f5-83a1-4e93-9e91-3a6c4bee7874"}}

Political experience: I have never held an elected office; I served as secretary of the New Mexico Human Services Department under former Gov. Gary Johnson

Relevant life experience: I was raised in rural New Mexico by a single mother on Medicaid and learned perseverance and grit from hardship. As a graduate of NMSU, I rose through accounting roles to become COO of Lovelace, one of New Mexico's largest healthcare providers. I later founded Ultra Health, growing it into the state's leading medical cannabis provider, creating hundreds of jobs, and serving thousands of patients. I brought my expertise to public service as secretary of the New Mexico Human Services Department under former Gov. Gary Johnson, deepening my understanding of healthcare access.

Have you ever been charged or convicted of a crime, including drunken driving?: No

Have you ever filed for bankruptcy or been involved in a bankruptcy proceeding, either personally or in business?: No

Have you ever been the subject of liens for unpaid taxes?: No

What sets you apart from the other candidates?

I will bring more than 40 years of business and private-sector experience to the Governor’s Office. I have worked on Main Street and Wall Street. I have operated companies with five employees and 5,000 employees. I have built businesses from the ground up. If you want the same tired old political platitudes, hire a politician. If you want innovative business solutions, hire a businessman like me.

What should the state government do to stem rising home sales and rental costs?

New Mexico has housing challenges, but it does not currently have a housing crisis. State government should work with counties to ease property taxes, should offer zero-interest down payment loans to first-time homebuyers, and should stop interfering with the contractual relationship between renters and landlords. If we allow the rental market to operate like an actual free market, costs will come down.

What are your views on data centers, and what should the state do to regulate them?

I support requiring data centers to build their own water supply through desalination, to build their own electrical supply, and to invest in co-generation. We should enforce uniform water rules on data centers — the same water rules other businesses must follow. I do not support a blanket ban.

If elected, would you ensure the continuation of taxpayer-funded childcare for all?

Gov. Michelle Lujan Grisham unlawfully and illegally implemented the current scheme of taxpayer-funded childcare. As governor, I will start the childcare discussion back at square one. I support childcare assistance, but it must be lawful and sustainable. Money should follow families. State funds must not and cannot go to non-citizens. Eligibility must be strictly examined. Income caps must come back into place. Child development experts must be consulted. The state must obtain a return on investment.

Do you plan to continue to follow the roadmap laid out by Gov. Michelle Lujan Grisham in her 50-year water action plan or would you plan major changes to the state's water policy?

We need much bolder action on water. The state should invest in construction of at least five major desalination projects across New Mexico. We have an ocean of brackish water we must exploit to ensure a vibrant future. We must also get tough on Texas and the lower Colorado basin states regarding Rio Grande water, Colorado River water, and other surface water. We need to educate farmers and ranchers on modern water conservation techniques and stop penalizing them for using less than their full appropriation.

Doug Turner

Age: 57

Party affiliation: Republican

Educational background: Graduated from high school in New Mexico, then earned a bachelor’s degree from American University in Washington, D.C., and a master’s degree from the Université Libre de Bruxelles in Belgium.

Occupation: Business owner

Political experience: Served as board chairman of Public Charter Schools of New Mexico for 15 years, eight years as chairman of the New Mexico Judicial Standards Commission. Currently serves on the Village of Taos Ski Valley Village Council and TIDD board.

{{tncms-asset app="editorial" id="e0106f57-acf9-469e-9a06-5623f74d2539"}}

Relevant life experience: I have spent my private-sector career in New Mexico building businesses that do work in more than in 40 countries, creating jobs and solving complex problems. My broad local, state, national and international experience has prepared me to bring accountability back to Santa Fe — whether that’s improving educational outcomes, making our communities safe, or standing up for the industries and small businesses that power New Mexico. I bring the kind of disciplined, results-driven leadership that can win across party lines.

Have you ever been charged or convicted of a crime, including drunken driving?: No

Have you ever filed for bankruptcy or been involved in a bankruptcy proceeding, either personally or in business?: No

Have you ever been the subject of liens for unpaid taxes?: No

What sets you apart from the other candidates?

I bring something none of my opponents can match: I’ve spent my career building things and fixing things — as an entrepreneur who grew a company from Albuquerque into operations in 40 countries, and as someone who has worked in high-stakes environments at the local, state, national and international levels. I’ve also worked on issues affecting New Mexico policy for decades, so I understand how government works and how it fails. My plan cuts personal income and gross receipts taxes, reforms our broken bail system, and rebuilds education around real accountability. I don’t owe anyone anything.

What should the state government do to stem rising home sales and rental costs?

The solution is fairly simple: We need to build more homes — both single- and multifamily — and reduce the cost of building those homes. Regulations are driving much of the increase in building costs and need to be dramatically reduced or streamlined so the supply of affordable housing can meet the demand. I have built homes and know that red tape is a killer. I will implement hands-on management and work with municipalities as appropriate to support streamline permitting and improve predictability. My administration will shift from a culture of broken legacy bureaucracy to one of accountability, ensuring that the state government works at the speed of business.

What are your views on data centers, and what should the state do to regulate them?

Data centers are coming to New Mexico whether we plan for them or not — the genie is out of the bottle. The question is whether we’re smart about it. I welcome investment that creates construction jobs and long-term employment, but every approval must come with binding, enforceable water-use commitments. Water is New Mexico’s most strategic resource, and no data center deal is worth trading our aquifers for. I will also ensure these large-scale projects don’t crowd out the small businesses that are the real backbone of our economy and the everyday jobs most New Mexicans depend on.

If elected, would you ensure the continuation of taxpayer-funded childcare for all?

Helping low-income working families afford childcare is an appropriate function of state government. Parents have to work, and costs have grown dramatically. But the benefit must go to parents who are working or actively looking for work and who genuinely need financial help. Government subsidies without accountability drive costs up, as we have seen in higher education. My priorities will be ensuring the benefit reaches those who truly need it, preventing abuse, and actively managing provider costs — because the goal is affordable childcare for working families, not an uncapped subsidy that enriches providers at taxpayer expense.

Do you plan to continue to follow the roadmap laid out by Gov. Michelle Lujan Grisham in her 50-year water action plan or would you plan major changes to the state's water policy?

I support the plan’s investments in wastewater treatment and water reuse, and I will enforce compliance with established water treaties. However, water is a zero sum game. Demand needs to be brought into alignment with supply. Aquifers need to be used to balance the demand and supply in surface water during short years and water use efficiency must be significantly improved. The 50-year plan needs stronger enforcement mechanisms and clearer interagency coordination. My administration will designate a single point of accountability for water strategy, grounded in science-backed assessments of aquifer health, reservoir capacity and long-term demand.
